Name: Ab'Ugartte
Description: Ab'Ugartte were a sentient race known to be secluded and unfriendly. Jak Sazz was a member of the race.

Name: Abron Mar's species
Description: This species first appeared[3] in the 1997[4][5] video game Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II,[3] added into the game in the Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith expansion pack[1] which debuted the year after the base game's release.[6]

Name: Aggoron
Description: The Aggoron were created by Timothy O'Brien for the article "The Gree Enclave," which was published in the magazine Star Wars Adventure Journal 8 in November 1995 by West End Games for Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game.[2]

Name: Agorffi
Description: The Agorffi were created for Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim, a West End Games sourcebook written by Simon Smith and Eric Trautmann and published in 1993.[1]

Name: Ahmar's species
Description: This sentient species is distinguished by the six horns found on their head, two protruding from the top, two from the side, and two from the chin. Their skin was varying shades of green, and they appeared hairless, oftentimes with yellow and then orange coloration around the ends of their horns, as well as yellowish patches surrounding some facial features.[1] The species was capable of both speaking Galactic Basic

Name: Aing-Tii
Description: In the new Star Wars canon, the Aing-Tii were first mentioned in the 2019 reference book Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary, written by Pablo Hidalgo.[1] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, the Aing-Tii first appeared in the story "Galaxy's Edge," published by West End Games in 1996 as part of The Kathol Outback, a supplement for Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game.[2]

Name: Alaphani
Description: Alaphani were a sentient species. They had plated and grooved gray skin with six light blue eyes between plates on their heads. Several grey teeth jutted outward from their mouth and had holes above them. Their heads were attached to their body by multiple thin connections. Alaphani had two segmented arms that ended in a hand with three long-nailed fingers each. They also had two legs and a foot for each. Members of

Name: Alazmec
Description: Hey. Never touch the buttons.

Name: Albingi
Description: The Albingi was a sentient species with copper-colored skin. The galaxy-renowned singer Ema Slake was an Albingi.[1]

Name: Allium's species
Description: This species was distinguished by their green skin, long fingers, tall necks, and enlarged craniums.[1]

Name: Alvadorjian
Description: Some of their physical characteristics included scalp plating that weathered over time and colorful eyespots on the neck and throat that spread with age. They also possessed a biological mutability that made cross-clan breeding difficult, and over time this trait had reduced the species population from 49 clans to seven.[1]

Name: Aman's species
Description: This species made their first appearance in the Star Wars: Visions Volume 3 episode The Bird of Paradise, directed by Tadahiro Yoshihira, written by both Yoshihira and Makoto Uezu, and produced by Polygon Pictures.[1]

Name: Amaran
Description: No. I am your father!

Name: Ame Llom's species
Description: Ame Llom's species was a sentient species[1] of Near-Humans,[2] a classification of humanoids that genetically differed from Humans.[4] Members of the species identified with the female gender[3] and could possess blue-green skin,[2] black hair, and brown eyes.[3]

Name: Anora's species
Description: The species first appeared in "Music Mayhem," the first part of the second episode of the Disney+ and Disney Junior animated series Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures' third season.[1]

Name: Aplocaph
Description: The Aplocaph were a sentient species preoccupied with finding love. Members of the species treated exotic locations and leisure time as an opportunity to find someone special. For this reason, during the time of the Galactic Civil War, the young Aplocaph Saduu Nagag took a cruise aboard the giant luxury liner Kuari Princess up the Relgim Run hyperlane. Almost hourly,[1] Nagag cleaned his skin until it gleamed to ensu

Name: Arakhyn
Description: The Arakhyns were humanoids, with four limbs. Tall and muscular, all male Arakhyns are instinctive and relentless.

Name: Armo Malou's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Arobernos
Description: The Arobernos first appeared in Rae Carson's Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition, the novelization of the 2019 film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, the final installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy.[3] It was later identified in the 2024 reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy.[1] The species was originally created for The Rise of Skywalke

Name: Asogian
Description: Asogians first appeared in the 1999 film Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, the first installment of the Star Wars prequel trilogy.[1] Their appearance was a homage to the title character of Steven Spielberg's 1982 classic film E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial to make good on a promise—after Spielberg included a Yoda cameo and Star Wars toys such as Boba Fett in E.T., George Lucas promised to include an E.T. cameo in

Name: Assorhian
Description: The Assorhians were a sentient species divided into two races: the black-skinned Ossapohans and the white-skinned Assopohans.[1]

Name: Voe Atell's species
Description: The species was humanoid, and sported bright red eyes and eight horn-like protrusions on top of the head, with blue or green skin.

Name: Atriik
Description: One Atriik was Mother Tunenep, a follower of the Path of the Open Hand. She lived to be more than 150 years old, an advanced age even for the Atriik.[1]

Name: Avrac Kefundee's species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pink skin with dark markings on their faces.[1]

Name: Axaleem
Description: Axaleem, albeit unidentified, first appeared in the first issue of the comic book miniseries Star Wars: Lando, which was written by Charles Soule, illustrated by Alex Maleev,[1] and published by Marvel Comics on July 8, 2015.[3] The species later got identified in the 2024 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy, which was authored by Dan Brooks, Megan

Name: Ayo Bons' species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their dark, grooved skin, as well as a series of bony horns running vertically across the tops of their heads.[1]

Name: Ayo Tam's species
Description: This diminutive humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their purple and mottled blue skin, as well as solid black eyes.[1]

Name: B'das
Description: The B'das were one of the New Republic's member species. Amamanam represented the B'das in the Senate.

Name: Babb
Description: The Babbs appeared in Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games, a 2002 young readers novel written by Jude Watson.[1]

Name: Feleen Bantillian's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Battrach
Description: The Battrach were a sentient species in the galaxy. During the High Republic Era, Jedi Knight Keeve Trennis claimed to have talked to a male Battrach gunrunner at Port Lennax when infiltrating the Nihil.[1]

Name: Bavakar
Description: The Bavakar were introduced in 2008's The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia.[1]

Name: Archyr Beil's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Bergamasque
Description: The Bergamasque were a sentient race. They had tentacles and stubby fingers. One member of the Bergamasque species was cantina owner and operator Salla C'airam.[1]

Name: Bhima Ook's species
Description: This small centaur-like sentient species was distinguished by their blue and purple skin, with four legs ending in hooves, as well as two hands, exposed nostrils, wideset black eyes, and a pair of long lobes dangling from their upper lip that resembled a moustache.[1]

Name: Bin Essada's species
Description: This sentient Near-Human humanoid species was distinguished by their light sensitive[1] pink eyes.[2]

Name: Bix Creo's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by gray-brown skin, with digitigrade legs and three-long-toed feet. Their arms were long, with their three-fingered hands dangling around their upper knees. They had elongated craniums, with pointed chins, downturned mouths, seemingly exposed nostrils, solid black eyes, and black hair.[1]

Name: Boma
Description: Bomas were large sentient species, standing at about five feet tall, and covered with brown fur. They did not speak Basic.

Name: Bon's species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale pink skin, white hair, and pale eyes, with craggy lines and dark mottled spots covering most of their body.[1]

Name: Borrim Shub's species
Description: This species was a sentient species that existed in the galaxy.[1] Borrim Shub was a member of this species.[2]

Name: Bozzie Baranta's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their purple and tan skin, as well as as well as their large heads and chins, wide toothy mouths, upturned noses, bright blue eyes, and pointed craniums.[1]

Name: Bratric
Description: The Bratric were a species mentioned by Jod Na Nawood when introducing the younglings Wim, Neel, KB, and Fern while they were disguised as the "Wise Elders" in order to gain entry to the Skull Ridge Mountain Hotel & Spa. According to Na Nawood the Bratric physiology did not allow them to speak or understand Galactic Basic. In order to reinforce this, the group spoke gibberish to each other while around the Lanupa hot

Name: Brebishem
Description: The Brebishem were a sentient species that had long snouts, wide leaf-shaped ears, and mauve-colored skin. Groups of Brebishem often huddled together, giving them the appearance of one larger organism.

Name: Brodihi
Description: The Brodihi were a sentient species that had a pair of horns and a long snout. Artifacts of the Brodihi were recovered by the Chiss Ascendancy from a wrecked starship and displayed in an art gallery, though the Chiss did not know the beings' exact origins. After analyzing the artifacts, which included Brodihi dinnerware, the Chiss cadet Mitth'raw'nuru "Thrawn" deduced that the species possessed a rigid hierarchy and

Name: Brogune
Description: The Brogune appeared in The Approaching Storm, a 2002 novel written by Alan Dean Foster.[1]

Name: Brownie
Description: As a species, Brownies tended to live in communities they referred to as kingdoms—a testament to their apparent inflated opinion of their place in the world—in small numbers, and appeared to be a predominantly male society as female Brownies went uncataloged along with the species' gender roles and reproductive systems.[1]

Name: Bufopel
Description: The Bufopel were a species of large-bodied sentient humanoids that were pale pink in coloration,[3] with two legs and two arms ended in stubby four-fingered hands. The species had no visible neck, instead having heads extruded from the upper portion of their thick, blubbery torsos, giving off the impression that they possessed a naturally hunched posture. These head was disproportionately small compared to their bodi

Name: Buktari
Description: This diminutive bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green skin, lekku, and breathing apparatus that covers their entire face, connecting to chest plates via extended tubes.[1]

Name: Cabrool Nuum's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Caphex
Description: The Caphex Bryn was a member of the Nihil marauders during the High Republic Era, serving under Melis Shryke in the Nihil Occlusion Zone.[5] The Caphex Dante was a member of the Jedi Order at the end of the Clone Wars and was selected as a candidate member of the Galactic Empire's Inquisitorius.[3]

Name: Carto Deeves' species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their sunken eyes, with light speckled skin, as well as prominent brows and other protruding features.[1]

Name: Caspus Pillar's species
Description: The species first appeared in the 2019 sequel trilogy film, Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker.[1]

Name: Celantes
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their hairless orange skin of varying shades, with dark mottled spots. They had elongated heads, with low-set mouths surrounded by pointed tendrils, as well as large, bulging, glassy black eyes, and a variety of short tube-like growths spread across their craniums.[1] Infant Celantes hatched instead of being produced via live birth.[2]

Name: Celestial
Description: The Celestials were ancient entities in the Force, the energy field that existed in all things, whose true nature could not be understood by the mortals of the universe. The Force was influenced under the Celestials' dominion, and three of them were known to have manifested into physical beings known as the Ones, Force wielders who were made up of the Father, the Daughter, and the Son.[2]

Name: Cemas
Description: The Cemas appear in the 1996 novel The New Rebellion, which was written by Kristine Kathryn Rusch.[1]

Name: Cephalon
Description: Cephalons were a long-lived sentient alien species that had ganglionic intelligence, with multiple sub-brains that sent information to a central brain. Cephalons had no visible sensory organs, and to communicate with other beings they had to use computers to translate their thoughts. They also perceived the galaxy in four dimensions, resulting in an inability to specify past or future-tense ideas.

Name: Cherff Maota's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their elongated faces, with small noses, pointed ears, and tall philtrums.[1]

Name: Chon Actrion's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their their wrinkled skin and sunken faces, with pointed chins, high-set eyes, naturally downturned mouths, wide, upturned nostrils, and large three-ridged head crest that strongly resembled those belonging to the Besalisk species, appearing hard and segmented, running up and over from their foreheads.[1]

Name: Cingulon
Description: Cingulons were a bipedal species with two legs and two arms. On their arms, they had two hands, with three fingers on each hand. They had large bodies covered in rocky, pebbly skin. Their heads had jagged flanges that framed the face, at the top of this structure was a distinct V-shaped dip. At the bottom of the face were two elongated orifice tubes, which lay along the sides of the face and followed the lower contou

Name: Clarodian
Description: The Clarodians[4] were a sentient species that were distinguished by the two horns protruding from the upper portion of their face.[3] Their skin was varying shades of green, tan, or even purple,[2] and they possessed solid black eyes, large noses, pointed ears,[3] and fangs,[2] as well as some brown hair on the top of their heads.[3]

Name: Clave Rollek's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue, scaly skin, that featuring a glittery sheen, as well as their white hair.[1]

Name: Coral-monk
Description: The Coral-monks were created by for the ambiguously canon roleplaying magazine Casus Belli 99.

Name: Corasgh
Description: The Corasgh were a sentient species who, under the New Order, established a nest colony located in the Koornacht Cluster of the Core Worlds. This colony was wiped out when its planet was conquered in the Yevethan Purge.

Name: Courataine
Description: Courataines first appeared in Han Solo's Revenge, a 1979 novel written by Brian Daley as the second volume of the Star Wars: The Han Solo Adventures trilogy.[2]

Name: Crotok
Description: The Crotok were a sentient alien species. This species' members included the pilot of a Helix-class light interceptor, Kizbon's Box, targeted by a gang of pirates led by Larken. The pirates mistakenly thought the pilot would be unarmed, but much to their surprise, the Crotok managed to outfight them using proton torpedoes.[1]

Name: Cthallops' species
Description: This massive species was distinguished by their green skin and massive tentacle-like appendages, as well as smaller facial tentacles that resembled facial hair, green eyes, and exposed nostrils.[1]

Name: Cusverian
Description: The Cusverians[4] were a bipedal humanoid sentient species that were distinguished by their pointed ears, upturned noses, and dark symmertical facial stripes, three straight ones from the tops of their eyes and around the sides of their heads, as well as one squiggly one extending down from the bottom of their eyes to their necks.[1]

Name: Cy Yunga's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their dark green skin, as well as their long necks with heads featuring only one wide eye with a green iris, and no other facial features.[1]

Name: Dabi
Description: The Dabi were a sentient species that had four arms that they could operate independently. A member of the species could speak Galactic Basic Standard.[1] In 40 ABY,[2] a Dabi worked as a bartender in Zerria's Bar on the planet Drall. When a visiting patron, the Mandalorian Mirta Gev, destroyed the speech circuit of the droid waiter Forre Musa, the bartender applauded her action, claiming the droid had been ruining b

Name: Deesh Crozli's species
Description: This diminutive sentient species[1] was distinguished by their unique metallic pressure-masks attached to a gas mixture monitors on their chests.[2] These masks had an almost organic appearance, featuring a defined nose and other detailed faux facial features, as well as bright orange lenses over the eyes and a translucent orange bulb extending from the mouth-area.[1] The masks and monitors periodically immersed the

Name: Ran Deezy's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Del Andue
Description: The Del Andues featured in rough draft of the script of the film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi, the final installment of the Original trilogy, and were set to appear in the opening scenes of the film. In the revised rough draft version of the script, the Del Andues were replaced by the Jawas Tweetu and Atetu.

Name: Dethro Glok's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their mottled orange skin, enlarged craniums, large brows covered in black hair, bulging noses, and eyes with red irises.[1]

Name: Dharus' species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Dorun
Description: Doruns were a sentient species. Its members had twin tentacles instead of arms, and eyestalks that could be moved independently of each other.

Name: Dostan
Description: Dostans were bipedal species with two legs, as well as two arms with three wide fingers each. They sported five tentacles from their faces, between their nose and their mouth, with two longer ones on the side commonly being wrapped around the sides of their heads and secured at their nape, while the other three were much smaller, dangling over their lips. They had orange skin with either red or green patterns on thei

Name: Draethos
Description: In the new Star Wars canon, the Draethos were first mentioned in the Nexus of Power, the 2016 Fantasy Flight Games Star Wars: Force and Destiny sourcebook,[2] and first appeared in the comic The High Republic (2021) 7, written by Cavan Scott, penciled by Georges Jeanty, and published by Marvel Comics[1] on July 28, 2021.[3] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, the Draethos were a species created by authors Kevin J. A

Name: Drall
Description: In the new Star Wars canon the Drall were first mentioned in the 2019 Fantasy Flight Games roleplaying supplement Gadgets and Gear.[1] The species originated in the Star Wars Legends continuity, where it was first mentioned in the 1980 novel Han Solo and the Lost Legacy, which was written by Brian Daley.[3]

Name: Drell
Description: Drells were a species known for building many starships used by pirates. They also often took up roles as mercenaries, pirates, and smugglers, their strength and large size making them a formidable foe. Blaster bolts would have no effect on the Drells' skin, simply bouncing off. Similarly, stun rounds would also bounce off and the vast majority of slugthrowers would not be able to pierce their thick hide.

Name: Duhma
Description: The Duhma were a rare sentient species that was divided into many tribes. The tribes identified themselves through use of ceremonial tattoos. The Duhma lived on the dark side of their planet and had lost their sight over time. As a result, they wore dark visors during their occasional journeys off their planet to protect their highly sensitive eyes.

Name: Dupei
Description: The Dupei were a sentient species. A group of Dupei monks resided on Nameel, and stole credits from a nearby Trandoshan tribe who tried to buy outlawed weapons from them. The Jedi Eno Cordova and Cere Junda were sent to Nameel to restore peace, and Cordova spoke with the Dupei, learning of their theft.[1]

Name: Duraceph
Description: Duraceph was a bipedal sentient species distinguished by their fleshy appearances, with blue and tan skin, as well as curved horns that extend from the sides of the head and reattach at the cheeks, all covered in a thick layer of hair.[1]

Name: Duu'ranh
Description: The Duu'ranh were a race of humanoids. This species' members included a young woman who was among the Fallanassi at the time of the Black Fleet Crisis.[1]

Name: Dwuni
Description: The Dwuni were a sentient species,[1] the members of which were generally half the height of an adult human.[2][3] Bipedal, they had two hands with three clawed digits each. A Dwuni's head was roughly one-quarter to one-third of its body size,[1] and it had an elongated shape with a flattened top that gradually sloped down toward their face.[2] The latter featured a large, sharp-toothed mouth with a prominent chin; a

Name: Dyclops
Description: The Dyclops were a sentient species who, despite their mostly humanoid body, were distinguished by their two heads, both of which featured a mouth, a single eye, and an ear. In the early days of the Galactic Empire, a Dyclops had joined the Red Fury Brotherhood, a pirate group led by Reddjak. The Dyclops served as a gunner aboard the Blood Brother, Reddjak's starship.

Name: Dynym Quid's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Dyplotid
Description: Dyplotids were a bipedal humanoid sentient species that were commonly brown, pink, or purple in coloration, possessing two legs, as well as two arms that ended in five-fingered hands. Dyplotids had large craniums, with flat faces that displayed no visible nose. These faces did, however, feature four keen eyes which granted them the ability to observe both the infrared and ultraviolet ends of the color spectrum. The s

Name: Echani
Description: The Echani were first mentioned in the current Star Wars canon in the 2021 novel The High Republic: The Rising Storm, written by Cavan Scott as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project's[1] Phase I.[3] The Echani originated in the Star Wars Legends continuity, where they first appeared in the 2003 video game Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic.[4]

Name: Ecorb
Description: Ecorbs were a sentient species that inhabited the Detral Pits. They had coiled attachments called hampas on their gelatinous heads and possessed red, pulsating blup sacks. Ecorbs were said to have grown hampas after excessively feeding themselves with a particular species of radiant orange and blue flower, a trait that was supposedly hereditarily transmitted to following generations.

Name: Eeusu Estornii's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished primarily by their pale skin and large black eyes.[2]

Name: Ellam's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Em'liy
Description: Em'liy were a sentient species. One Em'liy, Mikilanie, lived on Jedha during the High Republic Era.[1]

Name: Emente
Description: In the current Star Wars canon, an Emente appeared in the 2026 novel Star Wars Outlaws: Low Red Moon, which was written by Mike Chen.[1] The species originated in the Star Wars Legends continuity, where it appeared in the 2007 novel Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice, which was written by Karen Traviss.[3]

Name: Ensel
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their blue, pink, and tan skin, as well as their large craniums.[1]

Name: Equat
Description: The Equat were a species known to be epic poets.

Name: Ereesi
Description: The Ereesi were a species[1] from the Ereesus system[2] in the Outer Rim Territories.[3] The bulky Ereesi Argaz, a member of Marchion Ro's Sh'ear, attended a gala in the Palace of Jadalla on Waskiro[1] in 228 BBY.[4]

Name: Esh-kha
Description: The Esh-kha were an ancient alien species that inhabited the galaxy before being imprisoned by the Rakata on the planet Belsavis thousands of years before the Cold War.

Name: Espirion
Description: Espirion was a species that included the legendary Jedi Handi. During the rule of the Eternal Empire, researchers claimed to have discovered Handi's lost resting place.[1]

Name: Evereni
Description: The Evereni[4] were a sentient humanoid nomadic species, distinguished by their slate-gray skin and pitch-black eyes.[1] They once hailed from the planet Everon before the Great Storm ravaged their homeworld, which led to centuries of wars between the Evereni people and ultimately forced the survivors to flee into the galaxy.[4] During the High Republic Era, an Evereni male named Marchion Ro led a group of nihilistic

Name: Faiza's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their hunched postures and spindly limbs, with dark wrinkled skin, as well as elongated craniums, squinted eyes, and pointed chins.[1]

Name: Faksir's species
Description: Faksir's species appeared in the 2025 novel The High Republic: Trials of the Jedi, written by Charles Soule and published by Random House Worlds.[1]

Name: Felshi
Description: No. I am your father!

Name: Fenn Booda's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their white and black skin, with skinny limbs, three-fingered hands, and long necks. They had tall heads and long faces, with eyes white[1] or yellow[2] eyes featuring black, cross shaped pupils. Said eyes could change coloration between white[1] and yellow due to unknown means, and sometimes displayed a red glow.[2]

Name: Fetilian
Description: The Fetilians were a sentient species that required tibanna gas to breathe.[1]

Name: Fian
Description: The Fian were first canonically mentioned in the 2022 novel The High Republic: The Fallen Star, written by Claudia Gray for the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project.[1] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, the Fian species (plural Fia)[3] originated in the 1996 novel Before the Storm, the first book in the Black Fleet Crisis trilogy, written by Michael P. Kube-McDowell.[4]

Name: Filar-Nitzan
Description: In 231 BBY,[2] the Mirialan Jedi Knight Vernestra Rwoh mentally recalled the speculation regarding Nubarron's species, including the possbility of them being an unknown race of Filar-Nitzan, as she spoke with the Jedi Master on the space station Starlight Beacon.[1]

Name: Finhead Stonebone's species
Description: This species was first designed by Nilo Rodis-Jamero[3] for the 1983 film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi,[4] though this concept art would go unused.[3]

Name: Firrerreo
Description: The Firrerreo appeared in the current Star Wars canon in the 2022 novel Brotherhood, written by Mike Chen.[1] While drafting Brotherhood, Chen sought to include a diverse range of species in the Jedi Order. At the Lucasfilm Story Group's suggestion, a Firrerreo was included in the novel as a deep cut from the Star Wars Legends continuity.[5] In Legends, the Firrerreo were a Near-Human species from[6] the planet[7] Fi

Name: Flann
Description: The Flann were a sentient species that lived in the galaxy. One male Flann was a prisoner at a stronghold owned by Harra the Hutt.[1]

Name: Fneeb
Description: The Fneeb were created by Tom and Martha Veitch for the short story "A Hunter's Fate: Greedo's Tale," published in 1995 as part of the anthology Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina.[1]

Name: Fraii Wys
Description: The sentient alien race Fraii Wys was thought to be extinct for a very extended period of time. Then after nine thousand years, they reappeared in the galaxy and flourished. It was an extraordinary case brought up when considering the truth behind the disappearance of the Qella.[1]

Name: Freedi Pamular's species
Description: This bipedal species[1] was distinguished by their large builds and beige skin. They could speak the language Narkinian.[2]

Name: Frejuke
Description: Frejuke, albeit unidentified, first appeared in the theatrical film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, the final installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy.[2] which released on December 20, 2019.[3] The species was designed by concept artist Ivan Manzella.[4] The species later got identified in the 2024 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Gala

Name: Frog-dog
Description: The species first appeared in the 1983 theatrical film Episode VI Return of the Jedi, directed by Richard Marquand.[5]

Name: Frost Giant
Description: A number of them lived in caverns in the Mountain of Doom on the Forest Moon of Endor. They were ruled by a king, who captured Teebo and Wicket. They were terrified of the fire-breathing dragon-birds of Endor. When the two Ewoks helped them kill a dragon-bird that was harassing their mountain, the Giants helped the Ewoks locate a fire plant, a species unique to the mountain.

Name: Ftaaghn
Description: The Ftaaghn species appeared in the roleplaying adventure "La loi de l'Entropy" written by Christophe Debien and Patrick Bousquet for use with West End Games' Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game. The adventure was published in the eighty-third issue of the French Casus Belli magazine, released through October and November of 1994.[1]

Name: Fud Sang's species
Description: This bipedal[2] sentient species[1] was distinguished by their hunched, pudgy appearances, with large heads featuring bulging eyes, flat noses, and wide mouths.[2]

Name: Gab's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Galderian
Description: Galderians were average size and medium build, with "skin" that both looked and felt like tanned leather and was just as tough. Their facial appearance was goat-like, with long, curving horns protruding from the tops of their heads. They also had short tusks, which came out from the bottom of their mouths, pointing upwards, and upturned black noses. Much of their faces, including the tusks and horns, was as hard as b

Name: Ganimed
Description: A Ganimed appeared in 1995's The Mos Eisley Cantina Pop-Up Book, written by Kevin J. Anderson and Rebecca Moesta. The species was designed by Ralph McQuarrie.[1]

Name: Tomith Garbis' species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Garhoon
Description: The Garhoon were a species of vampiric sentients. Bail Organa presided over a court case between the Garhoons and their prey. At the end of the case, the prey returned to the Garhoons, saddening Leia Organa.

Name: Garoos
Description: Garoos had ear clusters which could be opened and closed, and spoke Basic with an odd, whistling accent. They breathed through purple-tinged gillis flaps.[1]

Name: Gas cloud
Description: A certain type of gas cloud in the galaxy was known to develop sentience.

Name: Gatha Elbaphay's species
Description: The species had green,[2] orange,[3] or tan colored skin.[1]

Name: Gatori's species
Description: The species appears reptilian and resembles that of a crocodile. Their necks are elongated with stubs along the spine. Their faces resemble a lizard and bear short tendrils at the top of their lips. Their hands and feet appear splayed and their tail is long and flattened.[1]

Name: Gen'Dai
Description: The Gen'Dai were a species of large, sentient beings. Their bodies were made up of a mass of regenerative tentacles, which allowed them to regenerate wounds quickly and, notoriously, be almost impossible to kill. Once a peaceful people, the Gen'Dai became violent nomads centuries before the Imperial Era and after the devastation of the Gen'Dai homeworld. The Gen'Dai did not tend to form lasting alliances with beings

Name: Gensang
Description: The Gensang were a sentient race. A spice thief of this species acquired a Sekotan Personal Starship at some point before 29 BBY from an unfortunate young Rodian who had been murdered by the guardsmen of the emperor-governor of Serpine.[1]

Name: Gharal
Description: The Gharal were introduced in Mike Chen's 2022 novel Brotherhood.[1] While writing the book, Chen struggled to create enough proper names for new elements of Star Wars, and so turned to fellow author Peng Shepherd for assistance. Shepherd in turn devised a list of various naming ideas, from which the term "Gharal" originated.[3]

Name: Ghawem
Description: The Ghawems were a sentient species of large, scaly beings who breathed methane. When in a methane-poor atmosphere, members of the species employed methane-spouting backpacks to make the air breathable for themselves. The Ghawems had enslaved another species, known as the Myills who they used for labor.[1]

Name: Ghular
Description: Ghulars were a sentient species. The Ghulars were enslaved by the Qhuloskians, forced to mine farium on Qhulosk.[1]

Name: Ginmid
Description: By 35 ABY,[4] Sozach—who had by then become a personal defense instructor sentenced to death after[2] a fatal accident in a marksmanship show that he had been putting on[1]—occasionally felt remorse over giving up the leadership of the Ginmid army,[2] most of whose soldiers had become retired.[1] At one point that year,[4] Sozach was present at the Spice Runners' Den in Kijimi City on[5] the Mid Rim Territories plane

Name: Brangus Glee's species
Description: The species was renowned for travelers. Brangus Glee, a Force-sensitive elder of the species with brown skin, originated from the planet Dor Nameth. Glee frequented casinos and spaceports during the Galactic Civil War.[1]

Name: Gloorag
Description: A Gloorag was a type of sentient species.

Name: Glothian
Description: The Glothians were a sentient species in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Glowen Faquidde's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their facial tendrils.[1]

Name: Gorach
Description: The Gorach were a sentient species.

Name: Gorg Singo's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green skin and large heads with wideset eyes.[1]

Name: Gorian Shard's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Gormaanda's species
Description: A sentient species, they possessed relatively humanoid features, with grayish-brown skin, gray hair, yellow eyes, and four arms.[1]

Name: Granik's species
Description: A member of this species, Granik, was a dispatcher who worked at Xanwan's freight kiosk on the planet Ferrix during 5 BBY. Granik spoke an alien language.[1]

Name: Grayla Stindy's species
Description: One member of the species was a dignitary who attended a party held by the House of Organa on Alderaan in 9 BBY.[4] There, the alien's words were translated for others by the protocol droid C-3PO.[1]

Name: Griddek
Description: The Griddek were an alien species known for their belligerence and rigid social structure. In this respect they were likened to Gamorreans.[1]

Name: Grueshite
Description: The Grueshites were an alien race. Arms dealer Jothel Merritt was a Grueshite.

Name: Gu-vandi
Description: The Gu-vandi were a sentient species. They were governed by the Gu-vandi Collective, and their area of space included the world Praven Prime, home to the L'Xing species. At some point by 3951 BBY, an HK-50 series assassin droid posing as a translator intentionally mistranslated a L'Xing language phrase, causing a civil war between the Gu-vandi Collective and the L'Xing. As a result of the conflict, the Galactic Repub

Name: Gueal
Description: The Gueal was featured in a segment called "The Gueal's Lament" in Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas, a book published in 1993.[1] Lucasfilm employee Leland Chee, who maintains the Holocron continuity database, has indicated that information contained in the book is non-canonical.[2]

Name: Guswan Askreeth's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their orange skin and black eyes.[1]

Name: Gwngi
Description: The Gwngi were a race or species which had a ceremonial dialect.

Name: Harf
Description: The Harf were a sentient species with an elongated snout and massive arms. They had goggle-like eyes that shined red. In 4 ABY, a drunken Harf was present in the Mos Eisley cantina when the Trandoshan bounty hunter Bossk and the Mandalorian Boba Fett had a face-off. Bossk won, with Fett paying him a large number of credits.[1]

Name: Hendanyn
Description: The Hendanyns were a species known for the elaborate death masks they created. These masks were unique in that they established a mental link with the wearer and preserved the wearer's memories. When a Hendanyn died, the mask was removed and the memories used by future generations.

Name: Hig
Description: The Hig were a slender, blue-colored sentient species. The Hig Ginbotham served aboard the Yavin under Wedge Antilles' command, and saw action against the forces of the terrorist Kueller at Almania. Another Hig was a supporter of the Diversity Alliance, a pro-alien, anti-Human organization, donating her ore hauler to the cause.

Name: Holwuff
Description: The Holwuff[2] were a sentient species in the galaxy.[1] One, Rogwa Wodrata was a senator.

Name: Houk
Description: Houks were a sentient species of hulking humanoids that had massive forearms and were capable of building heavy musculature.

Name: Huwawan
Description: The Huwawans were a bipedal humanoid sentient species distinguished by their tan skin, black hair, and large features. They spoke a language known as Huwawi with multiple dialects, and sometimes required a translation device to communicate with individuals who spoke Galactic Basic Standard.[1]

Name: I-Zellam's species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their tan skin, pair of long horns, pointed ears, large bulging eyes with small pupils, and elongated elephantine-like trunk.[1] They possessed a a number of powerful sensory organs clustered near their horns, ears, and trunks, grant them a keen sense for small details unnoticeable by most other sentients.[2]

Name: Ilwizzt
Description: The Ilwizzt were a sentient species with mahogany skin and a prominent trunk. That trunk flared and vibrated when they spoke. Brco was an Ilwizzt who attained the rank of major in the G'rho Defense Force. He was in charge in 1 BBY when a Ssi-ruuvi strike-force invaded the Wild Space planet. Despite ordering G'rho's Conjo fighters to attack the invaders, Brco and virtually all the other colonists were captured by the

Name: Imbat
Description: Imbats were a very large and bulky species, towering over 2.5 meters. Their legs were thick and muscled, with toes capable of grasping. They possessed disproportionately small heads with long, drooping ears. Their skin was leathery and tough.

Name: Immolanoid
Description: The Immolanoids were a sentient species that were known to combust.[1] By the year 18 BBY,[2] some Immolanoids had lived and commuted on Coruscant, the capital planet of the Galactic Republic. The Besalisk chef Dexter Jettster mentioned the species in the ice cream section of the menu for his CoCo Town Coruscant restaurant, Dex's Diner,[1] prior to its destruction at some point during the first two years of the Galac

Name: Inleshat
Description: Inleshat, albeit unidentified, returned to the current Star Wars canon in the third issue of the comic book series Star Wars: The High Republic, which was written by Cavan Scott, illustrated by Ario Anindito,[1] and published by Marvel Comics on October 12, 2022.[3] Scott later confirmed Inleshat's return to canon by clarifying Wozo's species.[2]

Name: Eero Iridian's species
Description: Flexible antennae distinguished members of a tall, spindly sentient species. The species also had feet, bright-yellow eyes, and shrill voices when concerned. They were members of the Galactic Republic during its final decades and were represented in the Galactic Senate by a senator. Eero Iridian, the senator's son, wished to replicate his father's success but twice failed to win the necessary election. Iridian later

Name: Iridonian
Description: The Iridonians, colloquially called Iridorians, were a warrior people with a particularly bloodthirsty reputation. Most galactic citizens feared them, for it was rumored that they would continue to attack a corpse even after the target was dead and turn against each other in battle when their bloodlust overwhelmed them. The Iridonians were rivals to the Echani and Mandalorian cultures, and members of the species were

Name: Jacipri
Description: The Jacipri were an ancient species recognized for their temple of deities and their vast collection of legends and myths concerning the birth and death of the galaxy.

Name: Jaing's species
Description: Members of the species had large horns that grew downwards out of the sides of their elongated heads and could live for thousands of years. The only known member of the species, Jaing, was a renowned Mandalorian training master who fought in multiple Mandalorian conflicts over the millennia.[2] The Mandalorian clans who served under Jaing adopted a crest that bore the skull of his species as a way to honor him. Due t

Name: Jashwik
Description: The Jashwik were a species of orange-skinned, jowled humanoids that hailed from the planet of Jashwa and were related to the Sullustans of Sullust. Cody Sunn-Childe was a Jashwik. He once theorized that his species was possibly a naturally Force-sensitive one.

Name: Jeodu
Description: Besides Dequc, other Jeodu included Eoodso, Traff, Chackra, and Clomv.

Name: Jinn Reeso's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their bluish-gray skin that grew lighter around their face and arms, as well as two-toed feet.[1]

Name: Jodi's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their dark green skin, large heads, and set of four pointed tendrils that extended down and out from the tops of said heads.[1]

Name: Judd's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Jumurra
Description: The Jumurra first appeared in "Destiny,"[1] the third episode of the television series Star Wars: The Acolyte, which aired June 11, 2024, on Disney+.[3] The species was first identified in the Databank entry for Naasa.[2]

Name: K'Armyn Viraxo's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by the large, fleshy horn protruding from their foreheads.[2]

Name: K'Jtari
Description: The k'Jtari were created by Paul Danner for Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy, published by West End Games in 1997.[1]

Name: Ka'aa
Description: The Ka'aa were a sapient species, known for wandering the stars. And were thought old enough to have seen the youngest stars of the Koornacht Cluster come into being. The called the Koornacht cluster, No'aat padu'll, which translated to the Little Nursery.[1]

Name: Kairos's species
Description: A humanoid species with chitinous features was native to a jungle planet. The species perceived there to be two worlds, their own and everything beyond it, and did not wish to come into contact with anything from outside of their planet. Its members wore wrapped garments and masks in order to conceal themselves and remain pure, additionally believing that doing so could heal their spirit if they did become unpure.[1]

Name: Kalai
Description: Among the galaxy's most strange inhabitants, little was known of them outside of speculation, rumors, fear, and very few facts.

Name: Kalkal
Description: Melvosh Bloor was a Kalkal.

Name: Keena's species
Description: The species was bipedal, with three-fingered hands and round heads. Their skin was purple, with darker patches under their eyes. They had large round eyes with black irises, small circular black eyebrows, thick black hair, and round pink ears. As children they were relatively short in height, smaller than most human children.[1]

Name: Kentian
Description: The Kentian were created for the Chinese web-novel The Vow of Silver Dawn and named as 肯特因人 (Pinyin: Kěntèyīnrén).

Name: Keredian
Description: Keredian species were first introduced in the mobile game Star Wars: Force Arena, after its 1.5 update on April 5, 2017.[8]

Name: Ketton
Description: The Ketton were a sentient species. The assassin Derrida was a Ketton who was active during the Galactic Civil War between the Alliance to Restore the Republic and the Galactic Empire.[1] In 1 BBY,[2] she needed a partner for an assassination job against the Alliance on the desert planet Tatooine. The Devaronian Kardue'sai'Malloc was aware of the job, but considered it too much work, arranging for the Kubaz spy Garin

Name: Kexing
Description: A Kexing appeared in the 2024 novel Temptation of the Force, written by Tessa Gratton and published as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project's[1] Phase III.[3]

Name: Kh'ymm's species
Description: The species was created for the character Kh'ymm, who appears in the live-action television series Star Wars: Skeleton Crew, where she was voiced by Alia Shawkat.[1]

Name: Khepi
Description: The Khepi were a theocratic species on the planet of Khepi, a group which worshipped the Stars. The Khepi existed in the Khepi system, moving to the Sayma Nebula after their world was predicted to be destroyed. Within a tomb constructed to preserve the species, they remained concealed from the galaxy until their tomb was reached by the Rokana Raiders.

Name: Kilmo's species
Description: This species was based off of an unused Dryden Vos concept sculpt originally created by artist Louis Wiltshire for the 2018 film Solo: A Star Wars Story.[3] The character Kilmo,[4] who was not featured in the final version of the film,[5] was first mentioned as a human in Solo: A Star Wars Story: Expanded Edition, written by Mur Lafferty,[6] though he was later retconned to be an alien member of this species when fir

Name: Kilotowan
Description: The Kilotowan species appeared in "The Queen's Bloom," a short story by Zoraida Córdova in the 2023 anthology young-adult novel The High Republic: Tales of Light and Life, released as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project.[1]

Name: Farn Klin's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Kogini
Description: The Kogini were a quadrupedal sentient species distinguished by their tan skin, hunched posture, massive arms and hands, and flat faces.[1] They possessed wide mouths, exposed nostrils, and small eyes that could perceive the ultraviolet spectrum.[2]

Name: Kooroo
Description: The Kooroo were a race of beings thought to have been the founders of the Fellowship of Kooroo.

Name: Korban
Description: The Korbans were a species of blue-skinned sentients that hailed from the planet Korba. Korbans possessed a head with green hair, two pink cheeks, and two large, pink eyes. Several of them worked as miners on Korba, mining kyber crystals in Korba's spires for the Jedi Order, which was deeply grateful for their work. No member of the species had left their world. The young Force-sensitive girl Aau and her father, Abat

Name: Kouru's species
Description: This near-human sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their pointed ears,[1] amber-colored eyes, and striking heads of hair, which were sometimes white in coloration.[2]

Name: Krawg
Description: The Krawg were a sentient species that lived in the galaxy. At some point during the reign of the Galactic Empire, a group of Krawg pirates raided credits from the planet Mykapo and later used them to pay the bounty hunter Sana Starros for a crate of Imperial blasters with the promise she would deliver them twelve more. However, she turned around and turned them in as the thieves to the Empire.[1] In 3 ABY,[3] the Ro

Name: Krish
Description: The Krish were first created for First Contact, a Star Wars Legends short story written by Timothy Zahn and published in Star Wars Adventure Journal 1 in 1994. The species were first mentioned in Star Wars in the 2015 novel Aftermath, written by Chuck Wendig.

Name: Kthoo-Ra's species
Description: This species first appeared in Doctor Aphra (2016) 10,[1] written by Kieron Gillen, illustrated by Kev Walker, and published by Marvel Comics.[6]

Name: Kuan'Mar
Description: The Kuan'Mar species appeared in the roleplaying adventure "Les Rats de Dwitzel" written by Jean Balczesak for use with West End Games' Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game. The adventure was published in the sixty-seventh issue French Casus Belli magazine, released through January and February of 1992.[1]

Name: Labbo
Description: Risi Amps was a Labbo wreck dealer and rebel who lived on Rumitaka in the Five Points system.[1]

Name: Lankorian
Description: The Lankorians were an alien species native to the planet Lankor VI. They were bipedal, with skinny arms and a head shaped like a sugar-loaf. Their planet was temperate and had had established a feudal-level civilization by the period of the Galactic Empire. Their planet came under the control of the Flight Unlimited Group, an Imperial-aligned corporation. While the Lankorians were largely ambivalent about FUG contro

Name: Latarzian
Description: The Latarzians were a sentient species in the galaxy. M'Kyas Love was a Latarzian.

Name: Lazu Lirona's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their blue skin[2] which possessed a scaly texture.[3]

Name: Lemmer
Description: The Lemmers were a species of small, sentient humanoids.

Name: Lepi
Description: The Lepi was a sentient species characterized by their lanky frame, long ears, and buck teeth. One notable Lepi was the smuggler Jaxxon.

Name: Letaki
Description: The Letaki were a species of sentient beings distinguished by eight tentacles, an egg-shaped head, four eyes, and a sharp beak. Two sets of gills allowed them to breathe in both air and water. They were able to focus their eyes on more than one object at a time, and to use their limbs to manipulate several items independently. As carnivores, Letaki gutted their dinners. The Letaki vocal range was higher than that of

Name: Lethagoe
Description: The Lethagoes were a mysterious hybrid species and a mix between Humans and Kalai. Lethagoes were Human in form but quite tall and thin. Nevertheless, they possessed a titanic strength that belied their thin frames. Their faces were thin and pale with a sharp nose, severe eyes and a jutting chin. Their appearance struck some as being quite birdlike, causing a theory to arise that the Kalai were an avian species.[1]

Name: Letrun Pay's species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their pale pink or gray skin,  large, boney heads, solid, dark blue eyes, and lack of visible mouths.[1]

Name: Lexlar
Description: The Lexlars were a species of sentient, hair-covered hominid. Odeon Farnish was a three-feet-tall, Basic-speaking Lexlar who worked as a customs inspector during the Galactic Civil War between the Galactic Empire and the Rebel Alliance. At one point, Farnish referred to another being as "hairless one."[1]

Name: Lisst'n
Description: The Lisst'n were a sentient species that continually shed their skin. They were active in the galaxy dating from the time of Xim the Despot, circa 25,000 BBY. According to legend, Lyechusas, Xim's court poet and the most renowned Lisst'n dramatist, penned three trilogies on the life of the Despot from this time. In the early years of the Galactic Empire, the University of Rudrig, the sole university in the Tion Hegem

Name: Llewebum
Description: The Llewebum were a sentient species with large bumps across their bodies, including a secondary series of bumps below their arms. The senator from Llewebum was severely injured in the explosion caused by Kueller in the Senate Hall on Coruscant.

Name: Loatha Sommover's species
Description: This large species was distinguished by elongated, caterpillar-like bodies, which featured various rolls, creases, and a thick, segmented tail. They possessed ten stubby limbs, with orang and brown mottled skin. Their locomotion could be assisted via repulsorlift platforms.[1]

Name: Lony Coleema's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Lucem
Description: Lucem were a species of humanoids found in the galaxy. Lucem were distinguishable by their nearly-translucent pale skin, white hair, and unique eyes. A Lucem's eyes were fully black with many small white pupils. The Lucem had control over their many pupils, which they could move around their eyes as a defense mechanism to cause vertigo in individuals perceived as a threat.[1]

Name: Luntobetha's species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species[1] could sometimes be distinguished by the old Bando Gora rebreather masks[2] that were attached to devices on their chests via a series of tubes.[1]

Name: Lupr'or
Description: The Lupr'or were a sentient humanoid species that moved to the planet Luprora after leaving their previous home many years before the Battle of Starkiller Base. During the hunt for Lieutenant Sol Rivas, Captain Phasma, TN-3465, and the BB unit BB-K8 encountered the Lupr'or and enlisted their help in storming the R'ora stronghold where Rivas was being held. After eliminating Rivas, Phasma abandoned the Lupr'or to the

Name: Luyal
Description: Luyals were a sentient species.

Name: Lyrobru
Description: Lyrobru, albeit unidentified, first appeared in "Announcement," the first season's seventh episode of the Disney+ television series Star Wars: Andor,[1] which aired on October 19, 2022.[7] The species was later identified as Lyrobru in the 2025 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide, written by Pablo Hidalgo.[3]

Name: M'ust
Description: M'usts were a species of subterrestrial-dwelling humanoids from a homeworld that was conquered by the Galactic Empire[1] in 3 BBY.[2] The M'usts had a religious culture that worshiped an eternally burning flame beneath their planet's surface, and generally followed a simple, peaceful way of life. After the Imperial conquest of their world, the M'usts came across Rebel warrior Cody Sunn-Childe, who shocked the natives

Name: Maghadi's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their pink and tan skin, with heads featuring at least three large lekku-like lobes, one hanging down their back and two over their shoulders, as well as a number of smaller tendrils dangling from their chins. Their noses had four nostrils, and the cheeks showcased a number of smaller holes of unknown purpose. They also had segmented foreheads, as well as large, bon

Name: Makurth
Description: The Makurth were a sentient species that lived in the galaxy. On Moltok, a Ho'Din settlement came under attack from a local Makurth boss, leading the Ho'Din to request Imperial muscle to avoid it turning into a full-fledged war.[1]

Name: Manikon
Description: Manikons ran on four legs and reared up to two when attacking. They had blunt, heavy feet that they used to bludgeon their enemy. They could also spew a stinging venom from their own eyes to temporarily blind their enemies. The venom could only be fired straight ahead.

Name: Manjtoo
Description: Manjtoo were a sentient species distinguished by their oddly shaped masks, with the ability to open and close their jaw.[1]

Name: Markul
Description: Darian Gli was a Markul.

Name: Maz Kanata's species
Description: A sentient species existed in the galaxy. The Force-sensitive being Maz Kanata[3] and an assassin were both members of the species.[4]

Name: Meek
Description: Meek.

Name: Melbu
Description: The Melbu were a sentient species that existed in the galaxy. They were distinguished by their respirator horns, which were connected to a breathing mask worn on the mouth. They also had reddish-brown skin and orange eyes.[1]

Name: Mennis Duren's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Midi-chlorian
Description: Midi-chlorians were intelligent microscopic life-forms that lived symbiotically inside the cells of all living things. When present in sufficient numbers, they could allow their host to detect the pervasive energy field known as the Force. Midi-chlorian counts were linked to potential in the Force, ranging from normal Human levels of 2,500 per cell to the much higher levels of Jedi. The highest known midi-chlorian co

Name: Miow Joon's species
Description: This small sentient species[1] was distinguished by Dug-like bodies, seeming to balance on their arms while they user their legs and feet to perform tasks. They had a fleshy appearance, with skin that was gray, purple, and tan, with small brown spots spready across their bodies. Their faces were asymmetrical, with jagged teeth, large crooked noses, pointed ears, and bulging eyes that featured black sclera and bright

Name: Miraluka
Description: In Legends, the Miraluka species first appeared in the comic Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1 in 1994, not appearing in Star Wars canon until August 30 2017[10] with the release of the comic Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu written by Matt Owens and illustrated by Denys Cowan. They were first mentioned in an article on the official Marvel website.[11]

Name: Mishtak
Description: The Mishtak people were New Republic members, some of which were part of the non-Bothan community present on Bothawui. In 19 ABY, during an anti-Bothan demonstration set off by the discovery of the Caamas Document, a Mishtak was hit by a blaster bolt. Though the blaster bolt was blamed by the crowd on either Han Solo or a Bothan clan leader named Rayl'skar, it was later discovered that the bolt was set off by an Impe

Name: Mizi
Description: The Mizi were a sentient species with long limbs, a long nose, and a lower susceptibility to radiation than most other species. As a result, Mizi often transported cargo that other species were unable to. The Mizi also preferred to remain nameless to strangers and additionally had a rivalry with the Orincans, another species from the same area of the galaxy.

Name: Modbrek
Description: Modbreks were a species of pale-skinned humanoids (or possibly Near-Humans). They had small noses and mouths, sharp facial features and large eyes. They were hairless, save for a mane of blue hair. At least one family of Modbreks lived in Mos Eisley on the desert world of Tatooine.

Name: Mokko's species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their large builds, red and beige skin, eyes with red sclera and yellow irises, large ears and chins, and interconnecting hard plating across their foreheads and brows.[1]

Name: Moldwarp
Description: Moldwarp were a bipedal sentient species. Moldwarp possessed two legs, as well as two arms ending in three-fingered hands. Their heads featured ridges lining down the back of the cranium, the species's snout took up a large portion of the species head. Moldwarp lacked visible ears.[2] Moldwarps could grow facial hair including on their chins[2] and upper lips.[3] A Moldwarp's skin could also posses scales on their fa

Name: Molemen
Description: Molemen were first identified in Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Attack of the Clones, a Star Wars Legends reference book.[2] They first became canon with their appearance in Star Wars: Complete Locations, which was released on September 27 2016.[3]

Name: Mongo Beefhead
Description: The Mongo Beefhead Tribe comes from a prototype action figure envisioned by Kenner for The Epic Continues, a canceled 1986 toy line. Designers created the original figure, named the Mongo Beefhead Tribesman, by taking the head of a "Squid Head" (Quarren) action figure, cutting off its neck, and turning it to face the front.[2] The head was then combined with the torso of a Squid Head figure, the arms from a "Hammer H

Name: Moorin
Description: Moorin was the name of a sentient species which was often characterized as being intelligent, but vicious. It was said that when meeting with a Moorin, music would help keep them agreeable.

Name: Morganian
Description: The Morganians were a species[1] from the Morgania system[2] in the Outer Rim Territories.[3] At least one Morganian attended a gala in the Palace of Jadalla on Waskiro[1] in 228 BBY,[4] and encountered the Sephi Tey Sirrek, who considered the individual to be "pretty" and "little". However, the Morganian informed the Harch Vulax of Sirrek's presence, complicating his mission.[1]

Name: Mostlaa
Description: Bill Smith created the Mostlaa species for the roleplaying adventure "Tales of the Smoking Blaster," published by West End Games in 1993 as part of the Star Wars Gamemaster Handbook. Jimmer, the Mostlaa, was illustrated by Paul Daly.[1]

Name: Mournish
Description: During the High Republic Era, a Mournish medic, Ambar, served a Pathfinder team on the Witherbloom.[1]

Name: Moyn
Description: The Moyn first appeared in "The Call," the thirteenth episode in the second season of the Star Wars Rebels television show.[1] The design for the species was based on a piece of alien bounty hunter concept art drawn by Ralph McQuarrie for Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back. Concept art of Yushyn for the Rebels episode was produced by artist JP Balmet.[5] The species was originally created for unfinished epi

Name: Mythrol
Description: The Mythrol were a sentient humanoid species with amphibious traits.

Name: Nagai
Description: Nagai were a sentient humanoid species hailing from the astronomical object Nagi. They had black hair, two red eyes, and pale white skin, along with pointy ears and unique face markings. The species was shown to be strong-willed and spoke in Galactic Basic Standard.

Name: Namara
Description: The Namara[5] were a sentient species distinguished by their black eyes and downward-turning horns that extended from the sides of their heads.[1]

Name: Navior's species
Description: The species was originally created during the pre-production phase of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, a film that was released during 1999. Detailed concept art was drawn by Terryl Whitlatch, but the character was cut from the film. The design was later reused for the character "Bullseye" Navior in the 1999 video game Star Wars Episode I: Racer, which brought the species into canon for the first time.

Name: Neidorian
Description: The Neidorians[2] were a sentient species[1] hailing from the world Neidoria.[2] This humanoid species was distinguished by their short stature, with round, fleshy heads and tan with with mottled brown spots, as well as specialized masks covering their faces.[1] By around 10 BBY,[4] one Neidorian, the cyborg Paghee'Dhe Dahglyn, performed as a podracer.[1]

Name: Newf Grayson's species
Description: The species was short and stout, with a compact build. It possessed two legs and two arms, with each hand ending in four fingers. The head had two large, widely spaced eyes that protruded slightly from the skull. The face featured a wide, flat snout with small, slitted, L-shaped nostrils and a broad, toothy mouth. The species had smooth, hairless skin that ranged in a pale grayish beige tone.[1]

Name: Nobah's species
Description: Hey. Never touch the buttons.

Name: Nobillian
Description: Nobillians, albeit unidentified, first appeared in the 2017 film Star Wars: Episode VIII The Last Jedi.[1] Although still unidentified at the time, Lucasfilm Story Group member Pablo Hidalgo nicknamed the species Glambug.[5] In the official ILM Immersive Discord server, the species was revealed to be named Nobillian by Lucasfilm Story Group member Leland Chee.[3] The species was named after Lillian Noble of Lucasfilm

Name: Nogratu
Description: Nogratu, albeit unidentified, first appeared in the second episode of the Disney+ live-action series Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi,[1] which aired on May 26, 2022.[3] The species later got identified in the 2024 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy, which was authored by Dan Brooks, Megan Crouse, Amy Richau, Amy Ratcliffe, Brandon Wainerdi, Dan Zehr, an

Name: Noti
Description: The Noti were an extragalactic, sentient, crustacean-like species native to the planet Peridea, a ringed planet located in the far galaxy, far away from the main galaxy.

Name: Nubarron's species
Description: The species which Nubarron was a member of appeared in the 2021 young-adult novel The High Republic: Out of the Shadows, written by Justina Ireland and published as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project's[2] Phase I.[3]

Name: Nugtosh's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their red[1] skin and skeletal limbs, with a short tail and glassy red eyes.[2]

Name: Numm
Description: The content of this Star Wars Legends article was cut.

Name: Octani
Description: Poe Dameron and Finn encountered the Octani Dhar Wat on a mission where they obtained parts for the Resistance from an Wat, who had them confiscated by First Order officers on a train.[1]

Name: Offen
Description: The Offen was a species that was relatively new to the concept of space travel by the time of the New Republic. They where led by a Offen queen who was 6,000 years old in 17 ABY.[1][2]

Name: Ogemite
Description: Ogemites appeared in the current Star Wars canon in the 2021 young-adult novel The High Republic: Into the Dark, written by Claudia Gray as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic multimedia project's[1] Phase I.[6] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, Ogemites were first introduced in the comic strip "Tatooine Sojourn." The strip was written by Steve Gerber, illustrated by Russ Manning, and released from September

Name: Okfili
Description: The Rebel team ultimately succeeded in its task.[1]

Name: Ongidae
Description: Ongidae were strong and disciplined, which made them ideal foot-soldiers.[1]

Name: Oodoc
Description: Oodocs were a sentient species known for their great size and strength but not their intelligence. They had spiked arms and backs and massive torsos. In 17 ABY, a number of Oodocs on Coruscant and the asteroid Skip 1 were affected by the Almanian Uprising, during which the Almanian dictator Kueller exploded droids across the galaxy to destabilize the New Republic.

Name: Opquis
Description: The Opquis were members of the New Republic. During the Caamas Document crisis, the Opquis senator took an anti-Bothan position, eventually resulting in the Opquis sending six warships to the Both system.

Name: Orthog's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their purple and tan skin. They possessed large builds, with large chins, with pronounced underbites, pointed teeth, yellow eyes, and long, pointed ears that stuck out from the sides of their heads.[1]

Name: Ozrelanso
Description: During the High Republic Era, the Ozrelanso Ratko served as a bodyguard for Sullustan Senator Tia Toon. His large and imposing build made his profession obvious, while Toon claimed Ratko was merely a secretary.[2]

Name: Padlom
Description: Rheena Sul and the Padlom species were created for the 2026 animated television series Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord,[1] with the species first being identified in Sul's databank entry on the official Star Wars website following the release of the show's third and fourth episodes[3] on April 13, 2026.[4]

Name: Palmopates
Description: The Palmopates were a sentient species. Two members of the species were found bickering on the planet Gilkahande.[1]

Name: Paonnid
Description: The Paonnid were a sentient species from the Paonid system. In 9 ABY, while wandering on a marketplace on the planet of Bimmisaari, the New Republic hero Han Solo spotted a being who looked like a Paonnid among the crowd.[1]

Name: Pappfak
Description: They may have had three sexes: at the wedding of Lady Valarian, musician Doikk Na'ts of the Modal Nodes observed three of them in what he described as a "prenuptial embrace."

Name: Partold
Description: The Partold were a sentient species.

Name: Pascwa
Description: The Pascwa were mentioned in "The Greel Wood Haven," a Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game source article written by C. Robert Carey and published in the May 1995 sixth issue of West End Games' Star Wars Adventure Journal magazine.[1]

Name: Passar
Description: Passars were a race equipped with natural venom.

Name: Pau'an and Utai precursor species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Peeples's species
Description: Peeples's species was a species of sentient beings with nineteen eyes and a snout-like proboscis. The species displayed a unique understanding of what was an appropriate moment to tell a joke. In 232 BBY, Galactic Republic Ensign Peeples was a member of the species who served aboard the Longbeam cruiser Aurora IX of the Republic Defense Coalition. He helped rescue the personnel of a damaged solar collection station d

Name: Pendago
Description: Pendago first appeared in the audiobook Dooku: Jedi Lost, written by Cavan Scott and published in 2019.[1] The spelling of their name was given in the script of the audiobook.[2]

Name: Penguin
Description: Penguins were a species of avians whose skin spanned a vast array of colors, including black, blue, green, orange, purple, white, and yellow. Unlike most bird species however, Penguins were unable to fly. They had multiple different distinctions such as bill, lekku, hair, fingers, skin patterns, and a flat pair of feet. Some Penguins had one or a few of these distinctions, but every Penguin had a bill and flat orange

Name: Phantidean
Description: Phantideans were a sentient species that existed in the galaxy. They had pink skin and large tusks.[1]

Name: Pikaati
Description: The Pikaati were a bipedal sentient species whose heads had two large, bulbous cranial lobes and breathed hydrogen cyanide, a gas that was poisonous to humans. They created Pikaati armor, a rare black armor that was able to withstand blaster fire, which was confiscated from them. A full suit of Pikaati gear ended up in the possession of Thetis Quelton, the owner of Quelton Fabrications, who complemented the Pikaati o

Name: Pikpik
Description: Pikpik were a diminutive rodent species. They had rat-like qualities including a long, hairless tail and large ears. They were covered in gray and brown fur and had a zig-zag shaped snout.[1]

Name: Pili Walde's species
Description: This tall sentient species was distinguished by their orange, scale-covered skin and large builds,[1] capable of standing more than 1.82 meters in height,[2] with big, three-fingered hands, broad shoulders, and lumpy heads covered in sporadic hairs. Their heads were wide and relatively flat, with no visible no visible mouths, ears, or noses, and eyes that were sometimes covered by specialized goggles attached to thei

Name: Plattahr's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Poldt
Description: The Poldts were skinny, long-snouted humanoids who had thin limbs, a flattened head.[2] Because of their lanky appearance, they bore a superficial resemblance to the Gungans of Naboo,[1] while their heads more closely resembled[2] that of the Geonosians, an insectoid species from Geonosis.[3]

Name: Polosich
Description: The Polosich were a sentient species with velvety skin of yellow pigmentation. Tyew Fordrod was a Polosich living during the Galactic Civil War. Fordrod was traveling aboard the Kuari Princess, a giant luxury liner, on a cruise up the Relgim Run when it was attacked by pirates while passing through the Maelstrom. Fordrod had booked accommodation on the Lido Deck and considered himself superior to anyone who traveled

Name: Pooba
Description: Pooba were a bipedal sentient species that lived in the galaxy. They were covered in furry blue hair and had multihued eye color. Two members of the species, Nubs and Barabo, served the Jedi Order as a Jedi youngling and a Jedi Master during the High Republic Era, respectively. Pooba spoke the language Poobian.

Name: Prae'tus
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue and tan scaly skin, with two pairs of arms, the upper possessing five fingers, while the lower only had three. Additionally, the species had solid black eyes, exposed nostrils, and a series of ten small orange spikes encircling their faces.[1]

Name: Prosslee
Description: The Prosslee were the former oppressors of the Forshul, a species indigenous to the neighboring Yminis sector. During the Caamas Document crisis, the Prosslee senator took a pro-Bothan stance in opposition to the Forshul. At least one Prosslee escort frigate participated in the Second Battle of Bothawui, fighting first on behalf of the Bothans, and then as part of the united New Republic fleet.

Name: Purple alien
Description: Although unnamed in 1991's Star Wars video game and its manual,[2] these aliens were identified in the Star Wars Hint Book.[1]

Name: Purple Ones
Description: The title of this article is a nickname, call sign, or alias.

Name: Puxian
Description: The Puxian, pronounced similar to "Pushian", were created for the Chinese web-novel The Vow of Silver Dawn and named as 普显人 (Pinyin: Pǔxiǎnrén).

Name: Q'Itane
Description: The Q'Itane were a sentient species that lived in the galaxy. Trusk Berinato, a friend of Bail Organa, was a male Q'Itane from Upper Etrega.[1]

Name: Qonet
Description: The Qonet were a sentient species formed from or by the Ahra Naffi and in turn beget the Qella.

Name: Quaraxian
Description: Quaraxian was a sentient species that aged very slowly and possessed significant intellectual prowess. By the time of the Republic Era, the species were virtually extinct, with the exception of individuals such as the Quaraxian female criminal Phaedra.[2]

Name: Quarren
Description: The Quarren were a bipedal squid-like species with four facial tentacles which protruded from their lower jaw. Their skin-color range included various hues of orange,[1] salmon,[5] tan,[8] pink,[4] and purple.[3] They had a pair of deep turquoise eyes, finned hands with suction-tipped fingers,[8] and small[6] tusks[9] on their mouth.[6] In the two long protrusions that extended from either side of their faces, Quarre

Name: Quill-head
Description: Quill-heads was the name by which Greedo identified a group of fellow bounty hunters he spotted in the Mos Eisley Cantina in 0 BBY.

Name: Quor'sav
Description: The Quor'sav first appeared in the 1977 film Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope.[2] The species was first canonically identified in the 2019 Star Wars By the Numbers video "Every Alien Species in the Original Trilogy".[3] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, the Quor'sav were a sentient species with avian and monotreme characteristics first identified in the Premiere Limited base set of the Star Wars Customizable Card

Name: Rangthang's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Rarririan
Description: The Rarririans were a sentient species. About 200 Rarririans lived on the space station Port Tooga during the Galactic Civil War.

Name: Rasta Alien
Description: Although unnamed in 1991's Star Wars video game and its manual,[2] these aliens were identified in the Star Wars Hint Book.[1] They are encountered by Luke Skywalker in various caves in the Tatooine levels of the video game.[2]

Name: Raze's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Reepayto
Description: Reepayto were an brown ant-like insectoid species was distinguished by their large size and features, including a pair of mandibles, a pair of antennae, glowing orange eyes, and multiple segmented limb.[1]

Name: Regineer Teed's species
Description: A species of sentient humanoid with yellow-and-blue skin and a pair of protruding tusks existed in the galaxy by the time of the reign of the Galactic Empire. Regineer Teed was a member of the species who, in 10 BBY, was part of the Cloud-Riders pirate gang, with the membership of the group being drawn from worlds victimized by the Five Crime Syndicates. Nineteen years later, several members of the same species were

Name: Rigellian (Kang)
Description: There was a species called the Rigellians too in the Star Wars Legends continuity, which appeared in the forty-eight issue of the Marvel Comics' Star Wars series published between 1977 and 1986.[3]

Name: Roon's species
Description: This frog-like humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale green and gray skin, with fleshy bumps covering their shins and heads, as well as vertical grooves running from their upper lips, around their mouths, and down under their chins. This species had massive heads, with wide mouths, rounded chins, and sharp cheekbones.[1]

Name: Rott Mercer's species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their human-like appearance, though they had extremely close-set eyes, and small bumps covering most of their heads.[1]

Name: Rughja
Description: Rugjha could become talented musicians, partly because they could play ten or more instruments at once. Among the most famous Rugjha musicians were Umjing Baab and His Swinging Trio (though Baab was considered a part of this trio, as he only had two fellow members in the band.) Though they could play instrumental music which humanoids could hear and enjoy, they did not sing to them, as their natural voices were inaud

Name: Rulan Prolik's species
Description: This species first appeared within the[1] 2003[3] video game Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic. If the player accepts the dark side quest, Revan kills Rulan in an alternate turn of events.[1]

Name: Rur's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Ryn
Description: James Luceno created the Ryn for the Legends novel The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial. Luceno also introduced the species to canon when he included a pair of them in Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel.

Name: Saadul
Description: The Saadul were a sentient species.[1] In 25 ABY,[2] a number of Saadul were displaced by the Yuuzhan Vong invasion of the galaxy, and ended up with numerous other refugees on the planet Ruan. The Ruan authorities created a central database keeping track of all refugees. While the list was sorted by species, the Saadul did not receive an entry in the main listing; instead, along with the Rybet and Ryn species, they w

Name: Sabetue
Description: The Sabetue were a species, genderless members of which were distinguished by a white skin. One Sabetue was a member of a gang of criminals in Coronet City of the planet Corellia in 13 BBY. That year, the Sabetue and their fellow gang members unsuccessfully attempted to purchase several vials of the coaxium hyperfuel from the human thief Han before attacking and then pursuing him through the city.
