Name: Sarkin Enneb's species
Description: Sometime during or before around 13 BBY,[2] a member of this species, Sarkin Enneb, lead a criminal group on the planet Corellia. Enneb had dealings with the Grindalid Lady Proxima, purchasing slaves from the White Worms' crime lord, including the human scrumrat Qi'ra, who, after being purchased by Crimson Dawn, would eventually return to kill the slaver[1] by 10 BBY.[3]

Name: Sarlacc
Description: Sarlaccs were dangerous, carnivorous creatures located on Tatooine and several other planets throughout the galaxy. It shared common ancestry with other species, including the rathtar, blixus, and vixus.[5] One sarlacc identified as the "almighty Sarlacc"[4] lived in the Great Pit of Carkoon, a depression of sand within Tatooine's Dune Sea, and served as one of Jabba the Hutt's favorite pets and a means of execution.

Name: Sawal
Description: The Sawals were greenish and scaly humanoids.

Name: Schiav
Description: The space pirate Jyn Obah was a Schiav.[1]

Name: Segredo
Description: The Segredo were a sentient species with the ability to manipulate the minds of others in order to appear invisible.[1] During the High Republic Era, there were few Segredo left in the galaxy.[2] By the time of the Clone Wars, Kreeda was the last of the Segredo, using her ability to shelter individuals from the dangers of the war.[1]

Name: Seneerian
Description: Seneerians were a sentient species in the galaxy. They were capable of smiling.[1]

Name: Setaran
Description: Setarans were a sentient species that had domed, ridged heads, pale skin, and gills running from the bottom of the neck to jutting cheekbones, framing two deep-set eyes. Setarans did not possess a mouth, and instead communicated with other sentients via telepathy. The species was known for their Force-related tracking abilities. Calar was a Setaran associated with the Path of the Open Hand.[1]

Name: Sethro Dagri's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their wrinkled skin.[1]

Name: Seuvhat
Description: The Seuvhat where a species that inhabited the known galaxy. A Seuvhat driver was seen on Ansion in 22 BBY.

Name: Shaull
Description: The Shaull were a sentient species that hailed from an unidentified planet. They possessed light-colored skin with patches of darker color on the wrist, forearm, and elbows. Shaull were hairless, but possessed a form of cilia-like growth on the backs of their heads, shoulders, and back. Their faces were very smooth and flat, without visible nostrils.

Name: Shorwilt
Description: Shorwilts[2] were a large humanoid species distinguished by their blue and tan textured skin, wide features, blubbery necks, large mouths, and wide-set eyes and nostrils. They had distinct prehensile tentacles that were normally attached to their backs and the tops of their heads, blending in with the rest of their skin, though they could peel back, detach, and extend them, able to grab and constrain things with the

Name: Sibettan
Description: Sibettans, albeit unidentified, first appeared in the first issue of the comic book miniseries Star Wars: Han Solo, which was written by Marjorie Liu, illustrated by Mark Brooks,[1] and published by Marvel Comics on June 15, 2016.[5] The species was later identified in the 2024 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy, which was authored by Dan Brooks,

Name: Aurra Sing's father's species
Description: This species had chalk white skin, sunken black eye sockets, and fingers with one more joint than a Human finger. Their long fingers were used for drawing blood. The lifespan of this species was much longer than that of a human. Aurra Sing's father was a member of this species.

Name: Siniteen
Description: They had pale skin and pupilless eyes. A notable feature of this species were their hairless, oversized heads, which resembled the lobes of a brain.

Name: Sirroning
Description: The Sirroning first appeared in Rae Carson's Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: Expanded Edition, the novelization of the 2019 film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, the final installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy.[3] It was later identified in the 2024 reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy.[1] The species was originally created for The Rise of Skywalke

Name: Sith
Description: Centuries before the Galactic Civil War, a member of the Sith species was part of the Darkseekers, a specialized sect within the Sith Order. During their attack on the Ascendant temple, the lightsaber-wielding Sith stabbed the Ascendant member Ilith, which ultimately led to his death.[1]

Name: Skeïtos
Description: They made starships.[1]

Name: Skilla
Description: The Skilla first appeared in the 2019 film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, the final installment of the Star Wars sequel trilogy.[3] The species was designed by concept artist Ivan Manzella under the title "Wizard 41".[4] The names of the Skilla and the first depicted member, Carib Diss, are tuckerizations of Scylla and Charybdis, a duo of sea monsters from Greek mythology.

Name: Skud's species
Description: Skud's species were a race of red-skinned humanoids. They had a frill of spines atop their heads, and what appeared to be naturally armored bodies. Their armor was not strong enough to stop a blaster bolt, however, as Skud learned at his peril when he was killed in a fight with Han Solo and Chewbacca on Orleon.[1]

Name: Slang
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Sledfrid
Description: Sledfrids were a sentient species that resembled jellyfish. Members of the species were notable for their lighter-than-air, gelatinous physiology, which allowed them to float, as well as their electric fronds that could be targeted at threats and were quite sticky. Sledfrids were incapable of speaking Basic without a vocabulator.[1]

Name: Slombo's species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their gray skin and large claws.[1]

Name: Slyte
Description: The Slyte were a species of sentient aliens. Nubblyk was a member of this species.

Name: Smoke Demon
Description: Smoke Demons were a dark side manifestation that could be summoned through Sith sorcery. They could metamorphose into their targets' worst fears and sought to vaporize their bodies. At some point following the Battle of Yavin, the S'kytri Dark Jedi named Kharys used a Smoke Demon to murder the smuggler Katya M'Buele.

Name: Smotl
Description: Smotls were a small, blue-green race. Ib-Dunn was a Smotl.

Name: Snook Uccorfay's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Sosear Latta's species
Description: A sentient species of brown-skinned bipeds with appendages on the side of their heads lived in the galaxy. Members of that species included Grock and Sosear Latta.[1]

Name: Squib
Description: Content approaching. Choose Your Destiny: A Smuggler Mission, Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 62 Guide to the Galaxy: Lotho Minor — Junk Planet, The High Republic (2023) 8–class.

Name: Squidge
Description: Long after the death of Luke Skywalker, two Squidge, Remoh and Otalp, found a broken C-3PO, known to them as the storyteller, in a cave. When Otalp was killed by the Vindar, Remoh picked up a lightsaber he found in the droid's wreckage and determined to bring hope to his people again.

Name: Ssty
Description: Ssty were a furry, sentient, bipedal species that possessed two arms and hands that bore tiny claws that acted like fingers. They were often thought of as cheaters.

Name: Stacchati
Description: The Stacchati were first mentioned in the current canon in the 2018 reference book Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary, New Edition, which was written by Pablo Hidalgo and David West Reynolds.[1] They originated in the Star Wars Legends continuity, where they were first mentioned in the Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary reference book, written by West Reynolds and released in 1998.[3]

Name: Strang's species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their large builds, pale cream and orange skin covered in dark mottled spots, thin white hair, silver pupils, and outward facing nostrils.[1]

Name: Strathen
Description: The Strathen were a sentient species whose first contact with another species was with the Duros.[1]

Name: Sugi
Description: The Sugis were a species that earned a reputation as being among the best arms dealers in the galaxy.[3]

Name: Susaut Volupicite's species
Description: Around 33 BBY, before the Clone Wars, a member of this species was an accomplice of Fissark Tozan. They were killed by Jango Fett when he came to collect a bounty on Tozan.[2]

Name: Swamp Maiden
Description: Swamp Maidens were an all-female sentient species, generally considered to be some of the galaxy's best exotic dancers.

Name: T'rinn
Description: A T'rinn first appeared in Han Solo and the Lost Legacy, a novel written by Brian Daley and released in 1980.

Name: Talid
Description: The Talid were first canonically mentioned in the 2019 audiobook Choose Your Destiny: A Podracer Mission, written by Cavan Scott.[1] In the Star Wars Legends continuity, the Talid species were originally introduced in the Star Wars: Episode I Racer, a 1999 LucasArts video game, and were native to Ando Prime and affiliated with the ancient Order of Dai Bendu.[4]

Name: Tallimarian
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their elongated craniums.[2]

Name: Taurill
Description: Taurill were a multi-limbed, furry species that shared a hive mind. At one point, Bandit Billie and Ratty-Ratty hired a group of Taurill to help them steal a starship, thinking their hive mind would work to their advantage.[1]

Name: Taymar
Description: During the High Republic Era, Svi'no Atchapat and her family were a group of Taymar from the Core Worlds planet Corellia who formed a musical group.[1]

Name: Teek
Description: The Teek species made its live-action canon debut in "Way, Way Out Past the Barrier", the second episode of Star Wars: Skeleton Crew.[4] Director David Lowery used puppetry to bring the character to life.[5]

Name: Tefaun
Description: The sentient humanoid Tefaun species had two arms and legs, a torso, a neck, and a head. One individual had blue skin, bulging eyes, and a ridged, disc-shaped head.[1]

Name: Theelin
Description: The Theelin were a rare sentient species recognizable by their brightly colored hair and spotted skin.

Name: Theran
Description: Therans were known as "listeners" because of their ability to hear the Tsils speak to them. Because the Therans listen, they were aware of what took place on Nam Chorios. Therans were in charge of keeping the drochs, carriers of the Death Seed, life suckers that sent their victims' life to Dzym on the planet. They accomplished this by protecting huge gun stations that were placed around the planet so that nothing lar

Name: Tholian
Description: Tholians were one of many species previously portrayed by the holovid actor Palleus Chuff prior to 20 BBY. The actor seemed to hold having played the species in high regard though why that was is unknown. Little is known about the Tholians, their culture, or even what they looked like. Considering Chuff's short stature, they were probably a diminuitive race if Chuff effectively portrayed one of them. They presumably

Name: Thranta Rider
Description: Thranta Riders, also known as wing riders, were humanoid sapients who tamed flying thrantas and rode them, performing aerial circus-like shows around the world of Bespin. They liked to paint or tattoo themselves with complex patterns.

Name: Tinian
Description: The Tinians were a sentient species that existed on a planet in the galaxy. They had very sensitive skin and considered blindness to be a gift and sight to be a disability.[1] Around 2000 BBY,[2] the Tinians forged the Tinian Codex, a stone carving with metal inlay which could be read by touch.[1] In 3 BBY,[3] while at a Senate Investiture party, Kleya Marki briefly described the Tinians while removing a listening de

Name: Tobar's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Tobek
Description: The Tobek were a sentient species. They rediscovered the planet Brath Qella after its destruction, renaming it Maltha Obex.

Name: Tof
Description: The Tof were a sentient humanoid species in the galaxy. They had green skin and were much taller than the average human. The species had potential to be quite strong as warriors, and could speak in Galactic Basic Standard. During the Fenril crisis, Anzati sorceress Reyna Oskure had several Tof soldiers in her employ on Nagi.[1][3]

Name: Tolgroat
Description: One Togroat was present on[1] the planet[3] Chandrila on the day of the Hanna City Flight Academy end-of-year family race[1] at some point between 8 ABY and 34 ABY.[4] Shortly before the race began, they wore white and red clothes and a tan hat with goggles. They worked at a stall selling[1] items touched by[5] the Jedi[6] Luke Skywalker.[5]

Name: Tor-Ohnahmi
Description: The Tor-Ohnahmi were a sentient Repto-mammalian species known as Torons to those more familiar with the race, originating from the planet Ohnahmi.[1] This species was naturally strong in the force. The Torons were all but wiped out by the Galactic Empire when they refused to be won or by them.

Name: Tordich Envlo's species
Description: This bipedal near-human species was better acclimated to warm climates than cold ones, requiring specialized protective clothing when in low temperatures.[2]

Name: Tosarvi
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by the six long[1] lekku[2] that extend down from their heads.[1]

Name: Tostonian
Description: A Tostonian appeared in "Part II" of the limited television series Obi-Wan Kenobi,[1] which was released on May 26, 2022.[4] The species was named in the episode's subtitles.[1]

Name: Tratlin
Description: The Tratlin were a sentient species. The species' members included Rallan, an elderly operative for the Alliance to Restore the Republic who served as First Officer of Sandwind Team.[1]

Name: Trizz Vezcro's species
Description: This lizard-like sentient species was distinguished by their short stature, with orange skin, and facial tendrils, and sharp teeth.[1]

Name: Trodatome
Description: The Trodatome were a sentient species. While they had no arms, they possessed prehensile sensory antennae and flippers. The Trodatome Klaud worked as a mechanic in the Resistance.[1] At least one Trodatome, Gido,[4] was present on the planet Koboh[5] in 9 BBY.[6]

Name: Trome
Description: Tromes bear some resemblance to the Seven Dwarves.

Name: Truishii
Description: The Truishii were a species of space-traveling people that were known for their large caravans. A group of Truishii traders whose caravan was shipwrecked following a Hyperspace accident became the original colonists of Trevi IV.[1]

Name: Trypto Buball's species
Description: This species was distinguished by their hunched postures, large, three-clawed hands, elongated necks, large yellow eyes, dark pink skin, and large moustaches.[1]

Name: Tsinimal
Description: The Tsinimals were a race of alien species. In 129 BBY, they conquered the planet Langhesa and enslaved the native Langhesi because they saw their biotechnology as an abomination to their gods. This led to a mass migration of Langhesi to other parts of the galaxy. However, piracy and war weren't opposed by their gods. The Tsinimals were described as "graceful and intolerant."

Name: Tulakt's species
Description: This short, yet large sentient species was distinguished by their dark green skin that featured a grooved texture, with four-fingered handss, as well as massive heads with wideset nostrils and eyes, wide mouths full of large, white teeth, and a pair of pointed barbs extending from their chins.[1]

Name: Tulli Mu's species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their varying shades of yellow skin, with four-fingered hands, long necks, thin eyestalks with tiny, bright yellow eyes situated at the tips, and no visible mouths, ears, or noses.[1]

Name: Tunda Ress' species
Description: This bipedal sentient species[1] was distinguished by their masks known as Mazanti face coverings.[2]

Name: Turan
Description: The Turan were a sentient species with orange skin.

Name: Turgle's species
Description: This gangly frog-like sentient species was distinguished by their beige, orange, and green skin, with thin limbs, rounded torsos, wide faces and mouths, and wide-set solid black eyes situated at the end of short eyestalks.[1]

Name: Turian
Description: The Turians were a species of Near-Humans. Senator Luralon Odaay was one. He came from the Limbala sector, but it is unknown whether or not this was where the species originated.

Name: Tursha
Description: The Tursha were a humanoid species distinguished by their head-tails and eleven-fingered hands. One of them was a bounty hunter who tried to capture Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker on their training exercise on Ragoon-6 using a stokhli stick to paralyze them. He was killed in 27 BBY with a fast poison injected in the neck.

Name: Tuut Orial's species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pink, textured skin, large noses, and pointed ears.[1]

Name: Ubasameir
Description: Ubasameir first appeared in Riders of the Maelstrom, a 1989 roleplaying game book written by Ray Winninger and published by West End Games for use with Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game.[1]

Name: Ubialla Gheal's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their elongated craniums.[2]

Name: Ug'Ggeran
Description: Ug'Ggerans were a sentient species. Cith Sninif was Ug'Ggeran.[1]

Name: Ugor
Description: Ugors were a sentient blob-like species that existed in the galaxy. During the High Republic Era, Archivist OrbaLin was the first member of the species to ever join the Jedi Order. HarrGick was a male Ugor that was in Coronet Spaceport on Corellia in 13 BBY, during the Imperial Era. Ugors could sprout pseudopods that performed the functions of limbs and, according to Tyro Viveca, they exuded an unpleasant smell.

Name: Ukanis
Description: Ukanis were a species of sentients with a round body. By 16 ABY, at least one female Ukanis could speak Galactic Basic Standard and had joined the Fallanassi.

Name: Ungrila
Description: The Ungrila[3] were distinguished by the several pairs of horns that grew above their mouths. They had black eyes, white hair, and brown[1] or white skin.[2]

Name: Unidentified anteater-like species
Description: This diminutive[2] sentient species was distinguished by their tan wrinkled skin, small black eyes, and elongated snouts.[1]

Name: Unidentified ape-like species
Description: This species first appeared in the 1999 film Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, written and directed by George Lucas.[1] When asked about this species, Leland Chee revealed that he had no additional information regarding them, going as far as to state he lumped them together with other unidentified species only ever seen in the far background of the Senate Building under one entry within the Holocron continuity

Name: Unidentified B-bel species
Description: This bipedal near-human species was distinguished by the pair of fleshy horns extending from the upper corners of their foreheads, as well as their natural heterochromia, with one eye being blue and the other being gold. It was not fatal for their horns to be removed.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald blue-skinned species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald diminutive species
Description: This diminutive bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their pale skin and elongated faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald mottled species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their mottled skin and enlarged nasal bridges.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald mustard-skinned species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their mustard-colored skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald red-skinned species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their red skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified bald species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their hairless heads, downturned mouths, and tan skin. They also had unique goggles with large, red, bulging lenses,[1] similar to the Jaluku.[2]

Name: Unidentified bald yellow-skinned species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their yellow skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified barbed light blue species
Description: This species first appeared in the first episode of the second season of the television series, Star Wars: The Mandalorian, directed by Jon Favreau.[1] This species was referred to by the nickname "Catfish" on set.[3]

Name: Unidentified barbed orange species
Description: In 9 ABY,[2] one member of this species visited Garsa Fwip's Sanctuary in Mos Espa,[1] a city on the planet[3] Tatooine. They were present when the Sanctuary was blown up by the members of the Pyke Syndicate.[1]

Name: Unidentified barbed pale species
Description: In 9 ABY,[2] one member of this species visited Garsa Fwip's Sanctuary in Mos Espa,[1] a city on the planet[3] Tatooine. They were present when the humans Boba Fett and Fennec Shand made their first visit to the establishment to speak with the Twi'lek owner Garsa Fwip.[1]

Name: Unidentified barbed red species
Description: Hey. Never touch the buttons.

Name: Unidentified barbed yellow species
Description: In 9 ABY,[2] one member of this species visited Garsa Fwip's Sanctuary in Mos Espa,[1] a city on the planet[3] Tatooine. They were present when the humans Boba Fett and Fennec Shand made their second visit to the establishment to speak with the Twi'lek owner Garsa Fwip shortly before the arrival of the Hutt brother and sister crime lord duo known as the Twins.[1]

Name: Unidentified beaked head-tail species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by orange skin, beaks, and head-tails.[1]

Name: Unidentified beaked species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange to gray gradient skin, as well as their beaked mouths.[1]

Name: Unidentified beaked two-toed species
Description: This bipedal avian sentient species was distinguished by their long dark gray hair and dark skin, with yellow beaks, as well as sharp talons of the same color. They had four-fingered hands and two-toed feet.[1]

Name: Unidentified bean-shaped species
Description: Some members of the species possessed pointed noses and exposed lower teeth,[1] while others had spherical noses instead.[2] At least one member of this species was present on the planet Coruscant[1] in 22 BBY,[3] while another visited Bezz Drexx's tower around the same time during the Clone Wars.[2]

Name: Unidentified big-eyed pointed-cranium species
Description: This diminutive bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their hunched posture, pink and brown skin, and elongated pointy craniums, with wide noses, bulging, dark eyes, and small fleshy fins on the back sides of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified black-and-white species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their white and black skin, featuring pink patches,[1] and mottled dark spots where the two primary colors met. They also had elongated craniums featuring four protruding knobs, as well as distinct masks covering their mouths and noses.[2]

Name: Unidentified blue humanoid species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified blue long-faced species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue skin and long faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified blue-and-tan species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue and tan heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified blue-masked species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale skin, large ears, and distinct glossy blue suits with masks that covered most of their faces and connected to devices on their backs via a pair of tubes. They also appeared to possess tentacle-like arms.[1]

Name: Unidentified blue-skinned short-legged species
Description: A humanoid species of blue-skinned and short-legged beings with conical heads existed, one of which was the notable Podracer pilot Toy Dampner. The species could exhibit red hair on their heads, and featured short legs in conjunction with long, skinny arms. A crowd favorite on the moon of Oovo IV, Dampner served as an example of his kind in the public spotlight.

Name: Unidentified blue-skinned species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified blue-upper-face species
Description: This species made the first episode of the 2022 televisions series Obi-Wan Kenobi, directed by Deborah Chow.[1]

Name: Unidentified bluish-green humanoid species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their bluish-green skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified boneless species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified brown furry-faced species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[3] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified brutish blue species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified brutish green species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their large bodies, green skin, and massive noses and aural cavities.[1]

Name: Unidentified bubble-eyed species
Description: This species appeared in the fourth episode of the first season of the live action television series Star Wars: Andor,[1] which first aired on September 21, 2022.[3] Leland Chee stated that he believed this species had been created specifically for Star Wars: Andor, and briefly compared them to another species in the show that reminded him of the Pink Panther,[4] though subsequently recalled that that species had ins

Name: Unidentified cheek-and-forehead-spiked species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin that grew a darker red shade around their eyes. They also had two pairs of horns, one set extending from their cheek bones and one from their lower forehead, as well as multi-pronged pointed ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified cheek-folds species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by the pair of large folds on each of their cheeks.[1]

Name: Unidentified cheek-spine species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their saurian appearance, with wide mouths full of sharp teeth, as well as six pointed facial spines, three extending back from each cheek.[1]

Name: Unidentified curved-beak species
Description: This species was first designed by concept artist Terryl Whitlatch for the 1999 film Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace as potential alien senators, though the designs would go unused,[3] later recycled in Dark Times 3 illustrated by Douglas Wheatley[1] and published on May 9, 2007.[4]

Name: Unidentified cylindrical-headed species
Description: This species appeared in the twenty-first episode of the Star Wars: Clone Wars animated series, which aired on March 21, 2005.[3]

Name: Unidentified dark-blue humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their textured, dark blue skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified dark-crested species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their tan skin, with rounded heads that sported beady, wideset eyes, flat ears, and a large, flat head crest that was black and brown in coloration.[1]

Name: Unidentified dark-eyed species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their tan skin, large black eyes, and exposed nostrils.[1]

Name: Unidentified diminutive green species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their green skin and short stature, with large heads, light colored eyes, and large, upturned noses with vertical nostrils.[1]

Name: Unidentified diminutive green-eyed species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their tan and pink skin, as well as their short stature,[1] with cylindrical bodies, downturned mouths with large lower lips, exposed nostrils, bulging green eyes that protruded from the sides of the head, and bushy tan eyebrows that at atop their cranium. They also possessed long, spindly arms ending in four-fingered hands and three-fingered toes with bright red toe

Name: Unidentified diminutive helmeted species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their short stature and four-fingered hands.[1]

Name: Unidentified diminutive pale species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their pale skin and short stature, with large heads, and eyes with yellow irises and large pupils.[1]

Name: Unidentified diminutive pointed-eared species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified diminutive round-headed species
Description: This diminutive sentient species was distinguished by their pink skin and large, rounded heads with wide, bulging dark eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified droopy-eared species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their green and large, fleshy heads, with pointed, drooping ears and tendrils dangling around their mouths.[1]

Name: Unidentified dust-breathing species
Description: This bipedal sentient species[1] was distinguished by their ability to breathe dust,[2] their distinct masks, and saucer-shaped helmets.[1]

Name: Unidentified earless humanoid species
Description: A sentient species, they possessed humanoid features, large builds, with pale skin, solid blue eyes, and a lack of visible ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified enlarged-cranium blue species
Description: This species made their debut in the ninth episode of the 2019 television series Star Wars: The Mandalorian, written and directed by Jon Favreau.[1] Another similar species appeared within the 2021 series, Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett,actually possessing visible ears[3]  like the original concept art for this species.[4]

Name: Unidentified enlarged-cranium purple species
Description: This species first appeared in the first episode of the television series, Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett, written by Jon Favreau and directed by Robert Rodriguez.[1] The species shared many traits with another species created for the first episode of the second season of the television series, Star Wars: The Mandalorian,[4] even possessing ears like that species' concept art while the actual species was earless.[5

Name: Unidentified exposed-nostrils species
Description: This species first appeared in Doctor Aphra (2020) 40,[1] written by Alyssa Wong, illustrated by Minkyu Jung, and published by Marvel Comics.[3] Jung revealed that his intention when designing this species was to aim for a "very classic Star Wars" feel.[4]

Name: Unidentified eyebrow species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their short stature, orange skin, black eyes, and bushy eyebrows.[1]

Name: Unidentified eyestalk species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin and a pair of stubby eyestalks.[1]

Name: Unidentified facial-apparatus species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their gray skin with faint blue stripes, sunken eyes that featured blue irises with red rings around them, and unique facial apparatuses that covered both ears, full of what appeared to be a thick liquid that ranged from green to red in coloration.

Name: Unidentified fan-crested species
Description: This species first appeared as a bust in the 2002 film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, written by both George Lucas and Jonathan Hales, and directed by the former.[1]

Name: Unidentified fin-headed beaked species
Description: This species first appeared as a bust in the 2002 film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, written by both George Lucas and Jonathan Hales, and directed by the former.[1]

Name: Unidentified fin-headed species
Description: This species was bipedal in form, with green skin. Its most notable feature was a narrow fin-like protrusion extending from the top of its head and extending backwards. It had yellow eyes.

Name: Unidentified flat-and-lobed-headed species
Description: The species appeared in The Story of the Faithful Wookiee,[1] a cartoon that aired on television as part of the The Star Wars Holiday Special,[3] which aired on November 17, 1978.[4] The cartoon was later included as an Easter egg on the 2011 Blu-ray release of Star Wars: The Complete Saga.[5]

Name: Unidentified flat-headed frog-like species
Description: At one point by 19 BBY,[2] a member of the species was in a settlement marketplace on the planet Korba, carrying a large tube on their back.[1]

Name: Unidentified fleshy hook-headed species
Description: This was first created as a potential design for the Besalisk Dexter Jettster/Legends in Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, created by John Knoll and team,[1] including Robert E. Barnes,[2] and presented to George Lucas alongside 23 other alien maquettes. While Lucas would end up choosing to combine two of the other maquettes to achieve his visions, this design, as well as five others, were photographed and

Name: Unidentified fleshy species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their blubbery bodies and flat faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified fleshy-flap-faced species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their leathery tan skin, drooping facial flaps,[1] and sharp teeth.[2]

Name: Unidentified fleshy-horned pointy-eared species
Description: The species first appeared in "Yuko's Treasure," a non-canon short film released as part of the third volume of the Star Wars: Visions series. The animated short, produced by Kinema Citrus Co., was released on Disney+[1] on October 29, 2025.[4]

Name: Unidentified floating species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified forehead species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their mottled orange skin, large foreheads, low, wideset eyes, and lack of visible noses.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-armed crested species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their red skin, with four arms, purple eyes, pointed teeth, a pair of large, fleshy, fan-like protrusions extending from their upper lips that resembled moustaches, and large, hard crests on top of their heads with blade-like growths protruding from the sides.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-armed four-nostriled species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their green skin and sharp features, with large ears that were pink on the inside, noses with four nostrils, and four arms, each ending in three-fingered hands.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-armed species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their purple hair, tan skin, and four arms.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-breasted horned species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their large builds and either orange and yellow[1] or purple, tan, and pink.[2] They possessed large lips and upturned noses that were both pink in coloration, as well as orange eyes with black pupils. The species possessed a pair of dark, curved, spiral horns extending up and back from the tops of their heads, and could grow a patch of brown[1] or purple[2] hair in

Name: Unidentified four-breasted species
Description: The species first appeared in "Way, Way Out Past the Barrier," the second episode of the live-action television series Star Wars: Skeleton Crew,[1] which premiered on December 2, 2024.[4]

Name: Unidentified four-eyed crested species
Description: This was first created as a potential design for the Besalisk Dexter Jettster/Legends in Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, created by John Knoll and team,[1] including Robert E. Barnes,[2] and presented to George Lucas alongside 23 other alien maquettes. While Lucas would end up choosing to combine two of the other maquettes to achieve his visions, this design, as well as five others, were photographed and

Name: Unidentified four-eyed Jawa-like species
Description: This species appeared in the twenty-first episode of the Star Wars: Clone Wars animated series, which aired on March 21, 2005.[3]

Name: Unidentified four-eyed lobed species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their white skin, four black eyes, and lobes that dangled down from the sides of both lips.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-eyed species
Description: The sentient species was bipedal in form, with a virtually bald head except a number of small growths around the crown. Members had four eyes—a set of larger eyes set lower in the head, with a second smaller pair set above these and closer together.[1]

Name: Unidentified four-eyed tendrilled species
Description: This species first appeared in the twenty-first episode of the Star Wars: Clone Wars animated series, which aired on March 21, 2005.[4]

Name: Unidentified four-horned species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their wide features, as well as two sets of two horns, one pointing up and one curving downwards.[1]

Name: Unidentified furry large-eared species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their fur-covered bodies, being brown and tan in coloration, with some lighter white hair growing from their faces. Members of this species bore canine-like traits, with elongated snouts and large noses, as well as long, wide, pointed ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified furry species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified goggled species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their pink skin, protruding mouths, and small eyes seemingly always protected by special goggles.[1]

Name: Unidentified gray near-human species
Description: This sentient humanoid near-human species was distinguished by their gray skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified gray wide-set-eyes species
Description: This species first appeared in the third issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified gray-skinned pointy-eared species
Description: This species first appeared in "The Missing Agent,"[1] the twelfth episode of the second season of the Star Wars Resistance animated television series, which aired on December 22, 2019.[4]

Name: Unidentified green cone-headed species
Description: These species had green or mint colored skin and had brown or green colored eyes. They also had cone-like skull.[1]

Name: Unidentified green flying species
Description: This species first appeared in the fifth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified green long-faced species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green skin, yellow irises in their eyes, fan-like ears, light hair and dark eyebrows, and elongated faces, with round mouths and exposed nostrils.[1]

Name: Unidentified green near-human species
Description: This species first appeared in the second issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified green thin-headed species
Description: This species first appeared in the fifth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified green yellow-eyed species
Description: This species appeared in the short comic story "Spy Girls" illustrated by Stewart McKenny and collected within Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 7,[1] released on January 31, 2007.[2]

Name: Unidentified green-and-tan-faced species
Description: At some point between 18 BBY and 3 BBY,[2] at least two members of the species were present on[1] the planet[3] Golak. One left the Golak Mahal by speeder bike as the human siblings Rani and Charuk arrived, the former being Force-sensitive. Another member of the species witnessed an Inquisitor confront Rani and was caught up in a scuffle when a sandstorm passed through the Mahal. Both members on Golak wore red turban

Name: Unidentified green-armed species
Description: This species first appeared in the first episode of the second season of the television series, Star Wars: The Mandalorian, directed by Jon Favreau.[1]

Name: Unidentified green-finned species
Description: Clode Rhoden, Madam Rhoden, and Buck were of this species.

Name: Unidentified green-skinned humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green skin and single strip of white hair on the tops of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified green-skinned species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their green skin, black hair, and black, pupil-less eyes. Their smooth-featured faces were distinguished by their lack of a protruding nose. Additionally, they possessed webbed hands with four digits each.[1]

Name: Unidentified greenish-blue species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished primarily by their greenish-blue skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified grey horned species
Description: The sentient species had a humanoid body plan, featuring two arms, two legs, one head, and an upright posture. They were grey-skinned and hairless, and their head featured a large muzzle, two small black eyes, a pug-like nose but no apparent external ear. Despite the size of their imposing jaws, they had a relatively small mouth filled with sharp, needle-like teeth. Two ridged cranial horns that bore a passing resemb

Name: Unidentified grey human-like species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their grey textured skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified groove-skinned species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their dark, grooved skin, orange eyes, and low-set mouths.[1]

Name: Unidentified hairy-eared species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their tan skin mottled with dark spots and stripes, as well as massive, pointed ears featuring long hairs protruding from within.[2]

Name: Unidentified hairy-humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their hairy heads and flat faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified head-tail species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their pointed ears, lone, elongated headtail, and orange or green skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified head-tendril species
Description: The species appears in the Star Wars: The Clone Wars television series' sixth season. The design was based off concept art for a female Sith that was cut from the film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones.[2]

Name: Unidentified head-tendrils species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their red skin and series of head tendrils that dangled from the tops of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified horn-row species
Description: This species first appeared in the 2010 comic book Legacy (2006) 48, written by both John Ostrander and Jan Duursema, illustrated by the latter.[1] Duursema had originally posted on an online message board in 2012 that she had intended for this Sith to be a Human with spike implants grafted on his skull, stating it as fact,[3] though this was eventually overruled when Leland Chee revealed on Discord that he treats th

Name: Unidentified horned humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their dark skin, curved horns that protruded from the sides of their heads, and a lack of visible ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified horned pointed-ears species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by the series of small fleshy horns running vertically across the tops of their heads, as well as pointed ears and a dark patch around their eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified hulking furry species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their hulking frames, hunched posture, pink, purple, and tan skin, long tan fur, and protruding brow.[1]

Name: Unidentified hulking horned species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their blue-gray and black skin, as well as tufts of fur of the same blue-gray color. They possessed large builds, with a number of horns on their heads, including two curved ones extending from behind their jaw, one sticking straight up from the top of their heads, two large, gangly black horns protruding from the back of their craniums, and an assortment of other s

Name: Unidentified hulking hunched species
Description: This large bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their blue skin and hunched posture.[1]

Name: Unidentified hulking plated species
Description: This imposing species was distinguished by large builds and features, green coloration, and hardened, spiky, armor-like skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified hunched lizard species
Description: This species first appeared within the[1] 1999[3] video game Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace video game.[1]

Name: Unidentified hunched species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their hunched posture, brown and pink skin, thick tails covered in stubby spines, and mouths featuring four fleshy, tube-like protrusions.[1]

Name: Unidentified jagged-cranial-spikes species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their blue skin,[1] yellow eyes,[2] and the jagged growths protruding from their cranium.[1]

Name: Unidentified jawline-horns species
Description: This species first appeared in the first episode of the second season of the television series, Star Wars: The Mandalorian, directed by Jon Favreau.[1]

Name: Unidentified jawline-spikes species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin, large nostrils, and small, light-colored spikes extending from their jaw.[1]

Name: Unidentified knobbly-headed species
Description: This species first appeared in the fourth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified Kullgroon species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their greenish-blue skin, sunken eyes, exposed teeth, and receded hairline.[1]

Name: Unidentified lanky species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their lanky bodies and green or purple skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified large grey humanoid species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their wide frame and grey skin, with large heads, wide mouths, and wideset eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified large white humanoid species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their stark white skin and large statures,[1] towering over individuals who were nearly 2-meters-tall.[2] The species also lacked visible nostrils, and their hands ended in fleshy, brown claws.[1]

Name: Unidentified large-armed species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their hulking stature, large features, wideset eyes, and brown[2] or green skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified large-eyed species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their brown skin, black hair, and large features.[1]

Name: Unidentified large-headed humanoid species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their enlarged heads and facial features.[1]

Name: Unidentified large-headed species
Description: This species was distinguished by their yellow skin, beady eyes. and large craniums with protruding brows.[1]

Name: Unidentified large-headed tendriled species
Description: The species first appeared in "Yuko's Treasure," a non-canon short film released as part of the third volume of the Star Wars: Visions series. The animated short, produced by Kinema Citrus Co., was released on Disney+[1] on October 29, 2025.[4]

Name: Unidentified large-upper-teeth species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[4] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-faced species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their large heads, with long faces,[1] pointed ears,[2] and elongated but blunt chins.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-headed alien species
Description: The species first appeared in X-Wing Rogue Squadron 8.[1] The Xenomorph of Alien featured directly in the humorous non-canon comic The Revenge of Tag & Bink where it appeared to be thumb-wrestling a member of the Yautja species from Predator in Chalmun's Cantina.[2]

Name: Unidentified long-headed black-eyed species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-necked dark-eyed species
Description: This species first appeared in the second issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-necked masked species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their long necks and unusual masks with a protrusion resembling that of a duck's bill.[1]

Name: Unidentified long-necked species
Description: The species was originally designed by artist Jake Lunt Davies for the 2019 film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, the final film of the sequel trilogy. Davies desired to push the form of creature design with this species, creating a concept that would hide the performer in the props carried on the creature's back. To operate the head, Davies envisioned the performer sticking their arm straight up through

Name: Unidentified long-snouted sunken-eyed species
Description: This species first appeared in the fifth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified lumpy-headed species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin and lump-covered cranium.[1]

Name: Unidentified mandible species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their blue skin, semi-furry bodies, and large mandibles.[1]

Name: Unidentified maroon species
Description: A sentient species, they possessed humanoid features, with maroon skin and pointy, layered cheek flaps.[1]

Name: Unidentified masked humanoid species
Description: The species made their debut in the 2019 film Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, directed by J.J. Abrams.[1] This species was originally designed by concept artist Jake Lunt Davies,[5] and was referred to by the production code "Wizard 54."[6] Davies personally considers the individuals who appear in both The Rise of Skywalker and Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi to be one in the same,[7] though it is unclear if t

Name: Unidentified mostly-blue species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their dark blue skin that grew lighter around their mouth and nose.[1]

Name: Unidentified mouthless humanoid species
Description: This species first appeared in Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters, published in 1994, specifically featured in an illustration by Mike Vilardi.[1]

Name: Unidentified mouthless species
Description: During the Cold War, one member lived aboard the Colossus on the planet Castilon[1] and liked to rest at Aunt Z's Tavern.[3] The alien later watched the descent of the station into the ocean in a corridor with Jooks.[4]

Name: Unidentified multi-chinned species
Description: This species first appeared as a bust in the 2002 film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, written by both George Lucas and Jonathan Hales, and directed by the former.[1]

Name: Unidentified nose species
Description: This diminutive bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale, fleshy appearances, with white hair, elongated heads, pointed ears, and large noses.[1]

Name: Unidentified noseless species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green and tan skin,[1] lacking visible nostrils, with black eyes, and an elongated cranium with fin-like growths covered in darkened stripes protruding from the sides.[2]

Name: Unidentified ogre-like species
Description: This large sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin, wide facial features, and pointed teeth.

Name: Unidentified one-eye-stalk species
Description: This species first appeared as a bust in the 2002 film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, written by both George Lucas and Jonathan Hales, and directed by the former.[1]

Name: Unidentified one-eyed bumpy species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their bumpy brown skin, white hair, single large eye with bright blue irises, and small spikes extending from their cheeks.[1]

Name: Unidentified one-eyed tentacled species
Description: This species first appeared in the fifth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified orange pointy-headed species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their orange skin and tall, pointed craniums with outward pointing ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified orange scaly species
Description: This species first appeared in the fourth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified orange species
Description: This species first appeared in the seventh episode of the television series Star Wars: Andor, directed by Benjamin Caron.[1] Leland Chee compared the appearance of this species to the Pink Panther.[2][3]

Name: Unidentified orange-haired humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale skin, orange hair, upturned noses, and sunken eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified ovoid-headed species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished by their elongated craniums and horizontal cavities on both sides of their necks.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale high-hairline species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale skin and receded hairline.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale mottled species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale skin with dark mottled spots on their forehead.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale three-eyed species
Description: This tall bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale pink skin, large rounded heads, and set of three eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale wrinkled species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their pale, wrinkled, tan skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale-blue humanoid species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their light blue skin with dark stripes on their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale-blue near-human species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale blue skin, as well as darker blue hair and eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified pale-skinned near-human species
Description: A sentient species, they possessed humanoid features, with pale blue[1] or white skin[2] and pink eyes.[3]

Name: Unidentified panther-like species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their black skin and fur, with felinoid features and blue eyes[1] that could turn yellow and orange if influenced by the Dark side of the Force.[2]

Name: Unidentified partially-red humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pale yellowish skin, with partially red faces, as well as a single strip of long dark hair extending from the tops of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified penguin-like species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their short stature, standing approximately 1 meter tall, and their avian appearance, possessing black, white, and yellow feathers, with orange feet and black talons, a red beak, and tufts of thin, long red and yellow feathers extending out from the top and back of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified pink droopy-eyed species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified pink hairy species
Description: This species was first designed by concept artist Sang Jun Lee under the title "Lemur Concept 11" for the 2005 film Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith, though the concept would go unused,[3] later recycled in Dark Times 4 illustrated by Douglas Wheatley[1] and published on July 18, 2007.[4] This was one of only a few "Lemur Concepts" chosen to receive maquettes molded by Robert E. Barnes, who stated they were

Name: Unidentified pink-and-white species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pink skin, with white circles around their eyes, as well as an additional white dot on their forehead.[1]

Name: Unidentified pink-haired enlarged-cranium species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pink skin and hair, as well as an enlarged cranium.[1]

Name: Unidentified pointy-cheeked species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their gray and red skin, as well as short fleshy points on their cheeks, chin, and the back of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified pointy-chinned species
Description: This species was first designed by Nilo Rodis-Jamero for the 1983 film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi as a potential design for one of Emperor Palpatine's Imperial Advisors, though they would go unused,[3] later reused for Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3, illustrated by Paul Gulacy[1] and published on January 13, 1999.[4]

Name: Unidentified pointy-eared partially-blue species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pointed ears and blue upper faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified pointy-headed species
Description: This species appeared in the fourth episode of the first season of the live action television series Star Wars: Andor,[1] which first aired on September 21, 2022.[3] Leland Chee stated that he believed this species had been created specifically for Star Wars: Andor, though he did not currently have access to any additional reference photos of it.[4]

Name: Unidentified pointy-teeth species
Description: This species was distinguished by their pointed teeth, cheek spikes, and pointed growths around their eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified porpoise-like species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their blue skin and elongated faces featuring large mouths filled with tiny teeth.[1]

Name: Unidentified primitive race
Description: An unidentified primitive race once traded with the crew of a tramp freighter.

Name: Unidentified prominent-nasion species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their prominent nose ridge and brow, as well as slightly elongated cranium.[1]

Name: Unidentified purple mouthless species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified purple near-human species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their purple skin. They also had yellow stripes situated horizontally across their faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified purple yellow-eyed species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their purple skin and hair, yellow eyes, and small barbs protruding from various portions of their faces. Instead of speaking, the species generally communicated via controlled scents.[1]

Name: Unidentified purple-and-white species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their purple skin with white markings around their faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified red-and-black species
Description: Hey. Never touch the buttons.

Name: Unidentified red-and-blue-faced species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their red and blue faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified red-masked species
Description: This diminutive humanoid sentient species was distinguished by what appeared to be dark red hair, as well as distinctive elongated glossy red masks that only revealed their eyes, as well as unique, spiky devices attached to the backs of their thick clothing.[1]

Name: Unidentified red-skinned near-human species
Description: A sentient species, they possessed near-Human features, with red skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified red-skinned species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified ridged-head species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by orange skin, low-set eyes, and ridged craniums.[1]

Name: Unidentified round-faced species
Description: A casino gambler who was a member of the species first appeared in "The Voxx Vortex 5000,"[1] the ninth episode of the second season of the Star Wars Resistance animated television series, which premiered on December 1, 2019.[4] Lucasfilm Animation artist Luke Harrington created concept art illustrations of the gambler dated to March 14, March 16,[5] and March 20 of 2018.[6] One of Harrington's works included several

Name: Unidentified round-headed species
Description: A bipedal humanoid sentient species, they possessed large, rounded heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified scaly-cranium species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their bright eyes, and elongated cranium, with a scale-like pattern running down from the top of their head down the back of their neck, while their front remained smooth.[1]

Name: Unidentified Selk species
Description: This bipedal species was distinguished by their red or orange skin, as well as their branching horns that grew with age.[2]

Name: Unidentified six-eyed rat-like species
Description: This tall species was distinguished by their rat-like appearances, with brown skin, gray fur, long fingers, elongated faces, and six eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified six-eyed tall-headed species
Description: At least one member of this species was present on the planet Coruscant[1] in 22 BBY,[3] while another visited Bezz Drexx's tower around the same time during the Clone Wars.[2]

Name: Unidentified slender four-armed species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their grayish skin, possessing six long limbs, two legs and four arms, with large appendages and flat faces.[1]

Name: Unidentified slimy species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their short stature and slimy pink heads.[1] These heads could potentially be detached from their bodies.[2]

Name: Unidentified small big-eared species
Description: This small bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their short stature, large appendages, tan skin, black eyes, sharp teeth, and large, pointed ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified small-eyed long-armed species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified small-mouthed species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their small mouths and tan skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified snouted species
Description: The species first appeared in the 2019 sequel trilogy film, Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker.[3]

Name: Unidentified species (Alderaan)
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their pointed brows, head stripes, and green hair.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Artemesium)
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their Light mint[2] or greyish-blue colored skin,[3] pale gray-green eyes,[2] sharp teeth, and ornate symmetrical grooves spread across their face.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Bazarre)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified species (Chandrila)
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their striped brown skin, bulging craniums, flat, curled ears, and protruding ridges around their eyes.[1] They also had the capacity to speak Galactic Basic Standard.[2]

Name: Unidentified species (Cipher Canyon)
Description: This diminutive bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their varying shades of blue scaly-like skin. They had rounded heads with spikes extending from the backs of their craniums, their faces featuring large eyes with large black pupils and gold irises, as well as exposed nostrils and wideset pointed teeth. They spoke an alien language.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Closed)
Description: This species made their debut in the first issue of the Star Wars: Obi-Wan and Anakin canon comic book miniseries,[1] written by Charles Soule, illustrated by Marco Checchetto, and published by Marvel Comics on January 6, 2016.[5]

Name: Unidentified species (Coruscant Eastern Spaceport)
Description: This species appeared in the fourth episode of the first season of the live action television series Star Wars: Andor,[1] which first aired on September 21, 2022.[3] Leland Chee stated that he believed this species had been created specifically for Star Wars: Andor, likening aspects of their appearance to that of a Gand, though unable to speak on the intent of those who designed it.[4]

Name: Unidentified species (Corvus)
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their diverse skin colorations, which included various pale color palettes such as pink, green, or blue, though usually featured some gray around their faces. Additionally, this species had large craniums, with exposed nostrils, eyes with black sclera and either blue[1] or yellow pupils,[2] and small aural openings on the sides of their heads. This species was cover

Name: Unidentified species (Daiyu)
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their low-set eyes and distinct coloration,[1] being either solid red[2] or splotchy red and white.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Fantanines)
Description: The natives appear in Marvel Comics' 2019 issue Age of Rebellion - Lando Calrissian 1.

Name: Unidentified species (First Light)
Description: The diminutive humanoid species was distinguished by their blue[1] or tan and pink skin,[2] as well as their short stature, large craniums, pointed ears, elongated snouts, large yellow eyes with small black pupils, and singular long patch of blonde hair that grew from the backs of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Fort Ypso)
Description: This species was first shown in the featurette "Scoundrels, Droids, Creatures and Cards: Welcome to Fort Ypso" that accompanied the home release of the 2018 film Solo: A Star Wars Story, where it was included among a maquette display of the gambling segment at Fort Ypso on Vandor,[1] though unlike the rest of the aliens in this display, this particular species never appeared within the final cut of the film.[3]

Name: Unidentified species (Four Masters)
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished primarily by their pointed ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Fox-Ear's underlings)
Description: This species made their first appearance in the Star Wars: Visions Volume 3 episode "Yuko's Treasure," directed by Masaki Tachibana, written by Natsuko Takahashi and Mika Tanaka, and produced by Kinema Citrus Co..[1] Concept art for this species created by Atsuko Nozaki depicts them as potentially being octopus-like, consisting of only a head and tentacles, which filled their humanoid-shaped suits.[3]

Name: Unidentified species (Genya)
Description: This species made their first appearance in the Star Wars: Visions Volume 3 episode "The Bounty Hunters," directed by Junichi Yamamoto, written by Hiroshi Seko, and produced by WIT Studio.[1] Unlike the other insurgents whose specific species were identified in concept art for the episode, this species was simply referred to as "Alien."[2]

Name: Unidentified species (Ghorman)
Description: This brown-skinned humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their sunken faces, large black eyes, spikes running across their craniums, and large ears set far back on the sides of their heads.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Glavis Ringworld)
Description: The species made their debut in the fifth episode of the 2021 television series Star Wars: The Book of Boba Fett, directed by Bryce Dallas Howard.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Golden Nyss Shipyards)
Description: During the final decade of the Galactic Republic, many members of this species worked at the Golden Nyss Shipyards, including Gzin, who received incoming cargo. In 33 BBY, the reptilian Yinchorri warriors infiltrated the station and carried out a massacre. The Yinchorri seized all the docked ships and then destroyed the shipyards.

Name: Unidentified species (Imperial Senate)
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their tan skin was reddish-brown markings, sporting an enlarged cranium, sunken eyes, and flat ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Imperial torching)
Description: Under the aegis of the Galactic Empire, several members of a sentient species were executed by stormtroopers. The species was humanoid, had red eyes, two short antennae, and was distinguished by a nasal structure composed of three pairs of breathing holes.

Name: Unidentified species (Jedi Temple)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified species (Keyorin)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified species (Leero's holodramas)
Description: Around that year,[2] an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing struggle between the Galactic Republic and the reconstituted Sith Empire tasked a companion with helping Leero facilitate sales of their works. The companion discovered the species and their veneration of Leero's holodramas, and subsequently the aliens happily traded their wealth for discs containing those works.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (mecho-organic builders)
Description: A species built massive mecho-organic vessels, including Ship, to fight in an extra-galactic war. At the end of this war one faction released a plague bomb. As far as Ship was aware, this wiped out the entire species.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Mina-Rau)
Description: This diminutive bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their mottled tan skin, orange hair, large heads, and big, wideset eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Nampi's guards)
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their purple skin, pair of eyes situated at the top of their faces, and a lack of visible noses.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Nentan)
Description: An alien species constructed a city on the planet Nentan. By the time of the Galactic Civil War, the city was a ruin in the Nentan wastes and no one knew anything about the original builders.

Name: Unidentified species (Nes Ukul's sacrifice)
Description: The Jedi padawan Nes Ukul sacrificed himself on a planet he did not know the name of, in order to save a sentient species whose language he could not speak.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Nevarro)
Description: This bipedal near-human sentient species[1] was distinguished by scale-like patterns on their faces, including a v-shaped split on their foreheads.[2]

Name: Unidentified species (Niamos)
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their thick, fleshy features, with pink skin, and spiky ridges on the tops of their heads as well as on their forearms.[1] They also possessed large feet, and elongated tails.[2]

Name: Unidentified species (Niima Outpost)
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their tall masks.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Open)
Description: This species made their debut in the first issue of the Star Wars: Obi-Wan and Anakin canon comic book miniseries,[1] written by Charles Soule, illustrated by Marco Checchetto, and published by Marvel Comics on January 6, 2016.[4]

Name: Unidentified species (Ord Mantell)
Description: This species first appeared in the[1] 1996[3] video game Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire video game. In game, when killed, members this species would produce a sound[1] similar to the screams of a Tusken Raider.[4]

Name: Unidentified species (Orvax IV)
Description: This species was first designed by concept artist Sang Jun Lee for the 2005 film Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith, though the concepts would go unused,[3] later recycled in Dark Times 3 illustrated by Douglas Wheatley[1] and published on May 9, 2007.[4]

Name: Unidentified species (Pasaana)
Description: In 35 ABY,[3] three members of the species were present on[1] the Expansion Region planet[4] Pasaana. They were shorter than adult Aki-Aki[1] and had black, vertical markings over each of their eyes.[2] The three aliens wore matching tan-and-dark-gray–patterned robes as well as tan-and-black headdresses that also covered their upper torso. The trio attended the Festival of the Ancestors in the planet's Forbidden Vall

Name: Unidentified species (Phorliss)
Description: This species first appeared in the fourth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Pseudograv projector)
Description: The species was introduced in the 2010 sourcebook The Unknown Regions, published by Wizards of the Coast for the Star Wars Roleplaying Game.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Republic Senate)
Description: This species first appeared in the 2002 film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones, written by both George Lucas and Jonathan Hales, and directed by the former.[1] When asked about another unidentified species, Leland Chee revealed that he had no additional information regarding any of the unidentified species only ever seen in the background of the Senate Building, going as far as to state he lumped them all to

Name: Unidentified species (seven-ringed homeworld)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified species (Shani)
Description: The species was mentioned in The Acolyte: Wayseeker, a 2025 novel written by Justina Ireland.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Suppoon's bodyguards)
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their brown skin, large stature and heads, and a lack of visible nose nor pupils.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Suppoon's entertainers)
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their yellow skin, with white circular marks on their thighs, as well as perpetually nearly-closed eyes, lack of visible noses, and a pair of thin antennae extending up from the top of their heads and through their hair.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Suppoon's guards)
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their green skin, elongated craniums, and four-fingered hands.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Suppoon's palace)
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their yellow skin, elongated faces, and four-fingered hands.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Svivren)
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[3] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Takodana)
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their tan skin, elongated necks, and large, hairless craniums.[1]

Name: Unidentified species (Tenoo)
Description: The species first appeared in "The Young Jedi," the first part of the first episode,[1] and "Nash's Race Day," the first part of the second episode, of the Disney+ and Disney Junior animated series Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures' first season,[8] which were shown exclusively on April 8, 2023, during Celebration Europe,[9] before a wider release on May 4, 2023.[10] Casey Straka was responsible for the design concept

Name: Unidentified species (Tirahnn)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified species (Vagaari captives)
Description: A species was captured by the Vagaari species.[1] In 27 BBY,[2] the Vagaari slaver fleet[3] imprisoned members of the species within zero-g plastic bubbles, using them as living shields during their attack on the Geroon species' homeworld. Force Commander Thrawn, the commander of the Chiss assault cruiser Springhawk, explained to his Human companion Maris Ferasi that unable to not kill them while fighting the Vagaari

Name: Unidentified species (Varkana)
Description: This species first appeared in "The Missing Agent,"[1] the twelfth episode of the second season of the Star Wars Resistance animated television series, which aired on December 22, 2019.[3]

Name: Unidentified species (Vincorba)
Description: The sentient species are named Mongo Beefhead in Star Wars Legends.

Name: Unidentified spike-crested humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their Orangish-brown skin, as well as their large head crests cover in two vertical rows of small spikes.[1]

Name: Unidentified spiky-eared spiky-faced species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their pointed ears, short spikes spread symmetrically across their faces, and orange patches on their skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified stalk-eyed species
Description: This alien species was reptilian in appearance, and appeared to average around 0.6 meters. They had distinctive stalk-mounted eyes, which appeared to be flexible and capable of looking in two directions at once.
