They also had a large underbite, with a number of small sharp teeth jutting up from their lower jaw.[1]

Name: Unidentified striated-skinned sunken-eyed species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their brown striated skin of varying shades, as well as sunken eyes, and distinct metal caps attached to circular portions of their craniums.[1]

Name: Unidentified striped-head-tail species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green skin and lone, elongated head tail which featured dark, segmented stripes. They also wore distinct facial apparatuses that covered their mouths[1]

Name: Unidentified sunken-eyed humanoid species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their light skin and dark, sunken eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified swimming species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified tall-cranium species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their enlarged craniums featuring a variety of deep grooves on the sides and back.[1]

Name: Unidentified tall-headed green-eyed species
Description: This species first appeared in the twenty-first episode of the Star Wars: Clone Wars animated series, which aired on March 21, 2005.[4]

Name: Unidentified tall-headed species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their dark skin tall heads with high-set eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified tall-horns species
Description: This tall bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their lanky frames, pale blue and pink skin, and elongated horns.[1]

Name: Unidentified tentacle-faced species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified tentacle-legged ornithoid species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified tentacled species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by the pair of prehensile cheek tentacles extending down from their ears and past their chin.[2] They possess mottled gray and blue skin. They also donned distinctive helmets that covered their entire face, though the purpose of these helmets is currently unknown.[1]

Name: Unidentified thin-chinned species
Description: This species was first designed by Nilo Rodis-Jamero for the 1983 film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi as a potential design for one of Emperor Palpatine's Imperial Advisor, though they would go unused,[3] later reused for Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3, illustrated by Paul Gulacy[1] and published on January 13, 1999.[4]

Name: Unidentified thin-pointy-eared species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their splotchy tan skin, with enlarged craniums, prominent nasal bridges, and and thin pointed ears that stuck outward.[1]

Name: Unidentified tusked species
Description: This bipedal sentient species was distinguished by their pair of tusks and tan skin.[1]

Name: Unidentified two-pronged-ears species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their light tan and pink skin, with sunken eyes, two-pronged ears, flat nasal bridges, and white "mutton chops-style" facial hair. The species' head shared some visual similarity[1] with that of the Tarlafars.[2]

Name: Unidentified wattled species
Description: This large humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their mottled brown skin and multi-fold wattle that extended from their chin to their chest.[1]

Name: Unidentified wavy-eared species
Description: This Near-Human sentient species was distinguished by their wavy ears.[1]

Name: Unidentified white-skinned species
Description: This white-skinned species was a sentient species of bipedals that possessed three-fingered hands, webbed toes and glowing blue eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified wide-chinned species
Description: This species was first designed by Nilo Rodis-Jamero for the 1983 film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi as a potential design for one of Emperor Palpatine's Imperial Advisor, though they would go unused,[3] later reused for Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3, illustrated by Paul Gulacy[1] and published on January 13, 1999.[4]

Name: Unidentified wide-mouthed species
Description: In 34 ABY,[2] a member of this species was chased down the streets near Canto Casino in Canto Bight on Cantonica by Resistance agents Finn and Rose Tico on fathier-back.[1]

Name: Unidentified wide-nosed species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their light skin, long faces, wide noses, and wideset eyes.[1]

Name: Unidentified worm-like species
Description: This sentient species was distinguished by their large size and cylindrical bodies.[1]

Name: Unidentified wrinkly humanoid species
Description: This species first appeared in the first episode of the second season of the television series, Star Wars: The Mandalorian, directed by Jon Favreau.[1]

Name: Unidentified yellow cone-headed species
Description: This species first appeared in the fifth issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified yellow large-headed species
Description: This species first appeared in the first issue[1] of the 1998[2] Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand series of comics, written by Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole, and illustrated by Carlos Ezquerra.[1]

Name: Unidentified yellow snouted species
Description: This species was first designed by Ralph McQuarrie for the 1983 film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi as a potential design for Jabba the Hutt's Gamorrean Guards, though they would go unused,[3] later reused for Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3, illustrated by Paul Gulacy[1] and published on January 13, 1999.[4]

Name: Unidentified yellow-skinned enlarged-cranium species
Description: This humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their yellow skin, as well as an enlarged cranium.[1]

Name: Unidentified yellowish-green humanoid species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their yellowish-green skin.[1]

Name: Urubaian
Description: The Urubaians were a sentient species that could be found on Urubai. They were a humanoid species distinguished by their teal and pink skin, as well as black hair and pointed ears.[1]

Name: Uteen
Description: Uteens were an eel-like species without hands. They were members of the New Republic.[1]

Name: Varovian
Description: Varovians were a sentient species from the planet Varovia. Naarok of the Varovian Order of Seers was a Varovian.[1]

Name: Vazooan
Description: Vazooans first appeared in "This Could Be a Real Adventure,"[1] the first episode of the Disney+ series Star Wars: Skeleton Crew, which aired on December 2, 2024.[2] Prior to the release of the episode, the individual Roona was pictured in the show's first trailer, which was released on August 10, 2024.[3]

Name: Veamon's species
Description: This sentient humanoid species was distinguished by their gray skin, solid blue eyes, and textured chins.[1]

Name: Veedaaz Awmetth's species
Description: This humanoid species was distinguished primarily by their textured, fleshy red skin, large craniums, and sharp teeth.[1]

Name: Verhandle's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Vicon
Description: The Vicon were a sentient species that grew to a height of only one meter. They had long noses, which twitched. Hran Beling was a male Vicon who was training at the Coruscant Jedi Temple as a youngling[1] in 89 BBY. That year,[2] the Dark Holocron was stolen from the Temple, and Beling conveyed this news to one of his contemporaries at the Temple, Dooku, speculating that there could have been an intruder in the build

Name: Vidara Jurness' species
Description: This bipedal humanoid sentient species was distinguished by their green, scaly skin, as well as a small black tuft of hair extending from the tops of their heads.[1]

Name: Vjun
Description: The Vjun were a mysterious species recorded in the library records of Darth Plagueis, possibly contemporaries of the Rakata.[1]

Name: Voegliss's species
Description: A sentient, predatory species that stood 1.7 meters in height had red-colored, hard, carapace-like skin, claws, a tooth-filled maw with large fangs, and hooded, glinting eyes. Members of the species were still young at the age of fifteen years.[1]

Name: Vojak's species
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Volkaran
Description: The Volkarans were a bipedal[2] humanoid sentient species distinguished by their green skin,[1] green eyes,[2] and lump-covered craniums.[1]

Name: Vollick
Description: Vollick was first mentioned in the 2019 audio drama Dooku: Jedi Lost, which was written by Cavan Scott and published by Random House Audio.[1] The species later made their first appearance through a vision in the seventh issue of the comic book series Star Wars: The High Republic also written by Scott, illustrated by Georges Jeanty, and published by Marvel Comics.[4] In 2025, Scott confirmed that Darth Krall was a me

Name: Volpai
Description: Volpai were a four-armed sentient species found in the galaxy. Notable Volpai included Robbs Ely, Tiggs Leo, and Moregi.

Name: Vonomi
Description: In 2020, Keeper of the Holocron, Leland Chee, stated on Twitter that "Clone Wars introduced Volpai and Vonomi in back-to-back seasons." This was in regards to a question about 4-armed species in Star Wars, and was the first mention of a species named the "Vonomi." Though not outright stated, given the context, with the Volpai first appearing in Season 2 of Star Wars: The Clone Wars and Rangthang debuting in Season 3,

Name: Vordum
Description: The Vordum were an alien species whose scent was considered repulsive by Humans.

Name: Vray
Description: The Vray were a gentle sentient species that was one of many forced to flee from the invading Yuuzhan Vong during the pangalactic Yuuzhan Vong War, started when they invaded the galaxy from another.[1] In 27 ABY,[2] Jedi ally and former New Republic leader, Leia Organa Solo, and her husband Han traveled to the Bilbringi Shipyards to request that its commander, General Muun, provide them with ships to form an escort t

Name: Vylla Tendeil's species
Description: This species was distinguished by their brown skin and fleshy appearance.[1]

Name: W'iiri
Description: The W'iiri were a six-legged sentient species with horn plating and pincers. A number of W'iiri worked for J'uoch, the leader of a criminal mining operation, on the planet Dellalt. They were present when J'uoch's mining camp came under attack from reactivated war-robots that had been part of Xim's Guardian Corps; three W'iiri died while defending the camp against the attack.

Name: Warthog
Description: The Warthog species first appeared in the first episode of the third season of Star Wars: The Mandalorian. The species was identified in the credits for the episode.[1] In the real world Warthogs (Phacochoerus) are a genus of pig with a similar looking head, native to sub-Saharan Africa.

Name: Waterspout
Description: The Waterspouts were a sentient humanoid species who were presumably composed of hydrogen compounds. A female waterspout was present as a manager at Crseih Station's Crater Lodge in 14 ABY where Lord Hethrir and other members of the Empire Reborn movement were lodging while trading in kidnapped alien slaves and paying a visit to the mysterious "faith-healer" Waru. Prior to a skirmish on the station, she reprimanded L

Name: Wayuning
Description: A Wayuning first appeared in the 2019 sequel trilogy film, Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker.[2] The species was identified as part of Star Wars: Batuu Bounty Hunters, an augmented reality video game launched at Walt Disney World[1] on July 27, 2022.[4]

Name: Wetakk
Description: The Wetakk first appeared in The Approaching Storm, a 2002 novel written by Alan Dean Foster.[1]

Name: Whirlwind
Description: The Whirlwinds were a sentient humanoid species who were presumably composed of gaseous compounds. A Whirlwind was present at Crseih Station's Crater Lodge in 14 ABY where Lord Hethrir and other members of the Empire Reborn movement were lodging while trading in kidnapped alien slaves and paying a visit to the Universe-traveling "faith-healer" Waru, whom presumably healed the creature.

Name: Wode
Description: During the Clone Wars, in which the Galactic Republic fought against the Confederacy of Independent Systems[2] from 22 BBY to 19 BBY,[3] the Wodes were liberated by the Republic's General Solomahal.[2] At some point between 2 BBY and 1 BBY,[4] a number of Wodes were present on the planet Bonadan at the same time that the smuggler Han Solo was there investigating an enslavement ring.[1]

Name: Xando's species
Description: A sentient species had horns, toothed mandibles, and glossy dark eyes. In 0 BBY, six members of the species who were part of the Norclu Order were seeking to undergo a pilgrimage from the planet Tatooine to the world of Norclune. They attempted to arrange transportation with the smuggler Han Solo; however, he refused the aliens' request.

Name: Xot
Description:
The Xot[4] were small-sized, with heads shaped like a pair of macrobinoculars. They had large feet, four-fingered hands that included an opposable thumb, two feelers above the nostrils, and two other feelers dangling from under their large eyes. Their skin varied in hue, from gray-blue[1] to pink.[2]
or even red.[3] Similarly, their irises came in a variety of colors, including green, pink,[1] and red.[3]

Name: Xylan
Description: The Xylans were an ancient species that built Kalonn, a planet-sized droid, for the sole goal to exterminate all life in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Xyrass
Description: The Xyrass were skeleton-like pygmies, with a hard, thick skin and very sharp teeth, similar to needles.[1]

Name: Yalor
Description: They had troopers.[1]

Name: Yao
Description: Yao were an intelligent species. Formayj, a friend of Han Solo and Chewbacca, was a Yao.

Name: Yarzion Vell's species
Description: Yarzion's Vell species appeared in the one-shot comic The Acolyte - Kelnacca 1, written by Cavan Scott, illustrated by Marika Cresta,[1] and published by Marvel Comics on September 4, 2024.[2] Before the comic's release, the species was first pictured at the Lucasfilm panel at San Diego Comic-Con on July 26 of that year.[3]

Name: Yesula's species
Description: The child Yesula and her father were members of a sentient species. At some point no earlier than 227 BBY, multiple members of the species were present on the astronomical object Veek when the home of Yesula and her father caught fire. They were rescued by Jedi Master Kelnacca and his Padawan, Yarzion Vell. Once they escaped, Vell returned to the building to save their droid, Zeekay, whose databank had holos of Yesul

Name: Ylvan
Description: The Ylvan were first pictured in The Mos Eisley Cantina Pop-Up Book, which was published in 1995. Kevin J. Anderson and Rebecca Moesta wrote the book and Ralph McQuarrie was the illustrator.[1]

Name: Yoda's species
Description: The species to which the Jedi Grand Master Yoda belonged was ancient and shrouded in mystery. Members of this species were rarely seen anywhere in the galaxy. The few members of this species seen in the galaxy were all Force-sensitive, and the species' homeworld and name were unknown.

Name: Yourellian
Description: Yourellians, also known as Urellians or Ureallians, were a race of large, slime-covered aliens with green skin and gills.[1] They hunted and trapped Wookiees on Yavin, and spoke the Wookiee language Shyriiwook.[2]

Name: Ysanna
Description: According to the legendary Qel-Droma Epics, the Ysanna were once the human Jedi survivors of the Cron Supernova, which devastated Ossus. The non-human survivors supposedly died out, and the remaining Jedi were forced to band together to survive the devastated wastelands of the planet.[1]

Name: Zekodoan
Description: Zekodoans were created for the animated series Star Wars: Young Jedi Adventures,[4] and first appeared in the episode "Kai's Bad Day."[1] The species was later identified as Zekodoan in the 2024 Dorling Kindersley reference book Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy,[3] which was authored by Dan Brooks, Megan Crouse, Amy Richau, Amy Ratcliffe, Brandon Wainerdi, Dan Zehr, and Kelly Kn

Name: Zeltron
Description: Zeltrons were a humanoid sentient species whose skin varied in shades of red. Whenever a Zeltron became angry, their skin turned a deeper red.[5] Ric Farazi was a Zeltron journalist during the High Republic Era.[2] Later in the High Republic Era, the Zeltron Klias Teradine was a Jedi Padawan on the planet Alaris Prime.[3] A Zeltron boy served as a youngling in the Jedi Order during the last decades of the Galactic Re

Name: Zeuol
Description: The Zeuols' facial features distinguished them from other sentient species. Set against their pale-white skin, their countenance sported a high brow, a mouth bordered by prominent pink lips, a long nose that ended in two oblong nostrils, and two pronounced ocular ridges. Located at either side of the nose, the eye sockets contained two large, ovular, pitch-black eyes rimmed with pink flesh. Coarse, ropey, off-white h

Name: Zilkin
Description: Zilkins were a small, green-skinned sentient species.

Name: Zinchorian
Description: The Zinchorians were a bipedal humanoid sentient species that were distinguished by the dark blue coloration covering much of their bodies, excluding most of their faces outside of a series of dark, symmetrical grooves.[1] These specific skin hues were caused by being removed from their native methane-filled atmosphere, though no other negative side effects have been identified as they possessed a versatile triple-lu

Name: Zixon
Description: The Zixon were a sentient species native to the planet Minfar. They were shorter than humans, covered in short and thick green fur, and had long pointed ears, a snout, and long-clawed hands. According to Glenna Kip's intelligence, the Zixon lived almost exclusively underground and were named after the squeaking noises made on first contact with outsiders.[1]

Name: Zoolli
Description: The Zoolli were a sentient humanoid species, distinguished from Humans by their longer noses. Judge Arabelle Lorteli was a Zoolli member of the Galactic Alliance judiciary. She was personally appointed by Chief of State Natasi Daala to the Court of Jedi Affairs; however, the Chief of State later publicly removed her from that post after allegations that the Zoolli had been mind tricked by members of the Jedi Order.

Name: Zornak's species
Description: Zornak and his companion belonged to a green-skinned species.[1]

Name: Zuzabol
Description: The Zuzabol species first appeared in the film Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens,[1] which was released on December 16, 2015.[5] The species were not identified in the film, but received a name in the non-canon video game Disney Infinity 3.0, which was released in conjunction with the film.[2] Despite the game being non-canon, story group member Pablo Hidalgo confirmed on Twitter that the names provided by the

Name: Aeradin
Description: Aeradin was a male professor of system geography and assistant dean at the Leadership School on Andara. He hosted the General Information Contest in the Great Hall of Learning.

Name: Aria
Description: Aria was a female Near-Human who in the city of Depatar in 31 BBY. She wanted to work in the Grande Game casino. To impress the owner, Kelko, Aria showed him her skills as a waiter, cashier and doorperson. Kelko instead believed that she was working for free, and decided not to hire her and enjoy her presence.[1]

Name: Geben Arrulis
Description: Geben Arrulis was a male Prince who ruled Bursoll II and was a member of the Near-Human species native to the planet.

Name: Reidi Artom
Description: Reidi Artom was a female Near-Human space explorer that discovered and named the Reidi Artom I, Reidi Artom II, Reidi Artom III, Reidi Artom IV, and Reidi Artom V systems. Additionally, she rediscovered the Cularin system (initially Reidi Artom VI) in 232 BBY.[1]

Name: Barsus
Description: During the SoroSuub Anniversary celebration in the city Tolea Biqua on the planet Genarius in 31 BBY, Barsus participated in a number of drinking contests in the Tolea Biqua franchise of Vanster Enan's Sop House. Barsus was able to win most of the contests by cheating, as he had an IV drip of purifying agents concealed in a pouch and connected to his femoral artery.[1]

Name: Putten Beatus
Description: Putten Beatus was a near-Human female. She was an outcast who found work as a customs inspector at the Mos Eisley Spaceport. She was different from a normal Human by her gray skin and large ears. Because of her looks, she was known to attract quite a few stares.

Name: Boog
Description: When Boba Fett was hunting down Abal Karda, he made his way to Port-Esta and spoke with the Port-Esta Queen's captain. Boog, meanwhile, rigged a crate of kneebs to fall onto Fett. Fett realized this and flew out of the crate's way with his jetpack. Fett then killed Boog.

Name: Relgar Carrae
Description: Relgar Carrae was a male member of an unidentified Near-Human species who worked as a pilot during the Galactic Civil War between the Alliance to Restore the Republic and the Galactic Empire. Rumored to have previously been a pirate, he was sponsored by anonymous members of the Hutt Cartel closely linked to Kaltho the Hutt to take part in the Cloud City Grand Prix, a cloud car racing event held around Cloud City on t

Name: Walda Ceen
Description: When CreedCon began construction of a race track through the jungles of the planet Cularin, Ceen was part of a group of construction workers who were tasked with building a bridge for part of the race course. However, they uncovered a mysterious cavern, which was home to an ancient creature, the t'salak, that had been imprisoned there by the Tarasin. The Dark Jedi Raik Muun traveled there and tried to free the creatu

Name: Jyss Cordam
Description: Jyss Cordam was a Near-Human alien computer technician and slicer who worked under Byro Ti'mere at Ti'mere's InfoServices. He was also one of the company's salesmen, and was InfoService's contact for corporate clients. Cordam's work led him to travel extensively throughout the Brak sector, meeting new and existing clients and opening new contracts for those seeking InfoServices' assistance. His work also takes him re

Name: Aga Culpa
Description: Aga Culpa was a male humanoid who ruled the moon of Nar Shaddaa during the years leading up to the Clone Wars. He was a puppet-ruler for the Hutts, crime-lords who owned the factories; his job was to keep the Hutts rich. They, doubtless, cared little about the methods he used to achieve his quotas, as long as they were met: whether Culpa, or Krayn―a slave-raider and the moon's true power-holder―claimed responsibility

Name: Dafi (criminal)
Description: Dafi was a female member of a green-skinned near-Human species and a member of a gang led by Human thugs Lemo and Sanda based on the planet Arcan IV during the Imperial Period. In 3 ABY, Dafi and the gang had a fateful encounter with Rebel agents Lando Calrissian and Chewbacca, who had come to Arcan IV looking for information on the whereabouts of their friend Han Solo. After luring them in with hints about the locat

Name: Sho'ban Do
Description: Because Do was born on Pitann, he exhibited the characteristics of those born on that world. He was short and barrel-chested, with large, muscular arms and legs, and dark, leathery skin. Since Pitann had a higher gravity than Dayark, he seemed to bounce when he walked on the surface of the Kathol Republic capital.[1]

Name: Jenson Drever II
Description: Drever had no interests outside of his business, and as such had no family or close friends. His closest relationship was with Madel Wharen, who was once on the Drever Corporation's Board of Directors. Drever and Wharen had very different philosophies, with the most notable example of this being Wharen's sympathies for the Alliance to Restore the Republic. As Drever did not share these sympathies, the two men had exp

Name: Tordich Envlo
Description: Envlo hoped that his captain would soon find work on a warmer planet, and in order to cope with the extremely cold temperatures on Vandor, Envlo spent a large sum of money on a dense kod'yok fur jacket that engulfed his entire body. He also wore an emergency vac-suit that covered his face and provided some insulation as well as a pair of hermetically sealed reactor gloves.[1]

Name: Bin Essada
Description: Moff Bin Essada was a Near-Human male who served as the Imperial military governor of the Circarpous sector. After learning from Captain-Supervisor Grammel that Princess Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker were present on Circarpous V, he contacted Darth Vader.

Name: Fay
Description: Fay was a centuries-old, female near-Human Jedi Master who was known to have had an intensely strong connection with the Force which allowed her to retain a youthful appearance. She was one of several legendary wandering Jedi Masters whose exploits were known throughout the Jedi Order.

Name: Quorlac Fornayh
Description: Quorlac Fornayh was a male Near-Human Sith Lord who lived over a thousand years before the Galactic Civil War. After the course of a war with the Galactic Republic turned against the Sith, Fornayh took shelter at his hidden fortress on the planet Yorgraxx. Over the next century, he channelled his efforts into the development of the coloi, a deadly, winged mutant rancor. By the time his creation was finalized, Fornayh

Name: Gauer
Description: Gauer was an assassin and agent employed by the Galactic Empire, and a conspirator in Grand Moff Trachta's plan to overthrow the Sith in 1 BBY.

Name: Cene Gilvent
Description: Cene Gilvent was a female Ocsinin swordsbeing.

Name: Regera Girawn
Description: Growing up on the remote and forgotten planet of Kruskan, Girawn lived in relative tranquility until she came into contact with a Sith holocron found by her servants. Being burnt by the device as it destroyed itself, she became mad, haunted by visions and terrifying her subjects. She was deposed, fled to the opposite side of the planet, and lived in secrecy, honing her Force-skills.[1]

Name: Gogol (patron)
Description: Gogol was a male humanoid criminal who haunted the Dor on Coruscant in the final years of the Galactic Republic, before the Clone Wars.

Name: Grecker
Description: Grecker was a member of the Closed who was participating in a battle on the planet Carnelion IV when Obi-Wan Kenobi and his padawan Anakin Skywalker crash-landed on the planet. While the two Jedi were talking to Pran and Kolara, he attacked the group with his airgun. While the rest took cover, Kenobi destroyed Grecker's ship with a saber throw. As his ship plunged to the ground, he leapt from the wreckage and was sav

Name: Greej
Description: Greej, an individual with three-fingered hands,[1] was once a caretaker[2] of an animal sanctuary. He wanted to return the animals to their natural habitats, but couldn't due having no money and no partners. Eventually Stroon, the owner of the Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie, imprisoned him[1] to keep from interfering.[2] He was placed alongside other animals in caves adapted as cages on Dandelo,[1] a planet in

Name: Gruna
Description: Gruna was the male Nessie captain of an Ithullan ore hauler, which carried valuable mutonium ore through the Stenness Node in the years leading up to the Great Sith War. Gruna's ship often came under attack by pirates, such as Finhead Stonebone and his gang, due to the hauler's expensive cargo. To counter this, Gruna paid local crime lord Bogga the Hutt to provide security while the ore hauler made its runs through t

Name: Hirsoot
Description: Hirsoot appears to be Human, but with pointy ears. Leland Chee, on the StarWars.com Message Boards, stated that his theory is that "all pointy-eared Humanoid species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi."[2]

Name: Kaa (Dark Jedi)
Description: A Near-Human of unknown origin, Kaa was nearly identical to her twin sister Lii in every respect except for the distinguishing vertical tattoos that adorned her face. Her sister possessed the same tattoos but in a horizontal manner. Both Kaa and her sister were adept in the Force, and they had obviously trained in its usage. However, whether they had received training from the Jedi Order or elsewhere is unknown. In a

Name: Macus Kayniph
Description: In 7 ABY, he first entered into a partnership with Grappa the Hutt. He acquired expensive Spaarti cloning cylinders and kidnapped Feena D'Asta, giving both to Grappa in return for gree spice shipments (of which Grappa only supplied the first). Grappa later killed Kayniph when he objected to Grappa murdering a Zanibar.

Name: Sundria Khartoor
Description: Lieutenant Sundria Khartoor was a female Near-Human working for the Royal Guard as a violent crimes officer during the Galactic Civil War.[1]

Name: Valik Kodank
Description: Valik Kodank was a female Near-Human who served the Sith Empire during the Golden Age of the Sith. The consort and apprentice of the minor Sith Lord Janton Belos, Kodank grew in power and founded her own Sith sect and constructed a Sith temple in a star system in the Abrion sector. However, circa 5000 BBY, while attempting a ritual to relocate her temple into the heart of the system's sun, Kodank was killed and trans

Name: Kolara
Description: Kolara was a female near-human who lived on Carnelion IV.

Name: Krox
Description: Krox was a two-headed sentient who gave Mungo Baobab the map to the Roon system in a cantina on the planet Manda. He left the cantina when two Imperial stormtroopers arrived.

Name: Lii
Description: Lii was a female Dark Jedi and the twin sister of Kaa. She was so similar to her sister that they were sometimes able to trick others into thinking they were simply one individual. They used this trick on the Confederate General Grievous at an unspecified time before the Battle of Geonosis and were able to gain his trust enough to be invited onto his starship. While there, they were able to steal Grievous' collection

Name: Lilly
Description: Lilly went to an academy on Ossus before transferring to Coruscant for unknown reasons. On her first day, she was instructed to make a sandwich with Cronah's help, and created a virtual whirlwind neatly whipping all the condiments into one, sauce included. This demonstration of Force power amazed everyone except Cronah himself, who criticized her for missing a condiment. Soon after, she beat Cyrus, an accomplished st

Name: Loi'e's son
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Gee Long
Description: Gee Long was humanoid or Near-Human with pointy ears. Leland Chee, on the Star Wars Message Boards, stated that his theory is that "all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi."[1]

Name: Gideon Longspar
Description: Gideon Longspar was a warlord and bandit who, in 3 ABY, terrorized the moisture farmers of Beheboth by raiding their farms for every drop of water they had and re-selling it on the black market. Longspar and his gang accomplished this after discovering how to manipulate and control the Tirrith, an empathic gaseous culture of hive-minded sentient particles that became key to his scheme. However, local farmer Darial An

Name: Lourdes
Description: When Lourdes was born, her entire population of her homeworld rejoiced. Her father was a much-beloved king, renowned for his fair governing style and his noble character. Though she lived a peaceful life, Lourdes was trained in swordsmanship by her father, whom she regarded as the finest swordsman in the system. She was attended by many retainers, one of them of a different species, named Skeeter.[1][2]

Name: Lynnori
Description: A typical tramp freighter captain Lynnori lived her days out among the stars traveling from stop to stop moving cargo and passengers around the galaxy. She was not opposed to the occasional smuggling job as well provided the pay was all right. She became romantically entangled with station owner Talandro Starlyte who repeatedly requested that she to quit her wandering ways but she repeatedly refused. Concerned for he

Name: Chizz Major
Description: Chizz Major was a male Near-Human sabacc ringer working for the casino of the Sanads of Rorkee hotel. He enjoyed his work as it allowed him to take advantage of underprivileged people. He was not beyond selling information.

Name: Bruce Mercy
Description: Bruce Mercy was a near-Human male space smuggler from a high-gravity world, who was associated with three other smugglers: Morph, Durquist, and Ptak-Sok. Mercy's ship, the Black Widow acted as their command ship. The smugglers attempted to steal the location of the Fool's Gold from Plako, but a group of freelance adventurers beat them to the legendary ship.

Name: Mira (Near-Human)
Description: Mira was a near-Human female Jedi Knight who served the Jedi Order during the final years of the Galactic Republic. When the Clone Wars broke out between the Republic and the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Mira invoked her Right of Denial and refused to take up the rank of General in the Grand Army of the Republic. Representing a group of like-minded Jedi, Mira was killed by Dark Jedi Asajj Ventress at a confere

Name: Jozzel Moffet
Description: Jozzel Moffet was a female near-Human handmaiden in the service of Malta the Hutt before the Battle of Yavin. Having taken the job in the hopes of seeking her fortune, she saw her chance to break away from her master and do just that after learning of the existence of the Yavin Vassilika. Moffet manipulated Malta, along with the Hutts Embra and Jabba, into a contest to see who could locate the Vassilika first—her tru

Name: Saalo Morn
Description: Saalo Morn was a male Near-Human Dark Jedi who abandoned the Jedi Order during the Inter-Sith Wars period.

Name: Zeela Nall
Description: General Orma Hundeen along with Zeela Nall, Nuri Pravada, Sheyne Tyndos and Leia Organa were on a mission on Elerion to recover the holocube map containing the location of Rebel main bases from Nescan Tal'yo, Hermos and Gogol.

Name: Nomma
Description: Nomma was a diminutive and heavily-jowled near-human male who served the Tarkin family in their home on the planet Eriadu during the childhood of Wilhuff Tarkin. On one occasion, he was ordered by Wilhuff's parents to remove their child's meal during a family dinner, after which the Tarkins questioned their son on what he would do if he were to lose everything. They then spoke about sending Wilhuff to the Carrion Pla

Name: Norufu
Description: Norufu was a Near-Human slave of Stenness Node-based crime lord Bogga the Hutt. Norufu served as the captain of Bogga's flagship, the Enforcer One.

Name: Noth
Description: Noth lived in the wilds of the planet Alvorine. When Krova the Hutt arrived with a Sith holocron, he found himself drawn to it and made plans to acquire it for himself.[1]

Name: Cabrool Nuum
Description: When Jabba presented Cabrool Nuum with Princess Nampi's converted Nuffin freighter to sell it to him, Jabba discovered that his old friend had gone insane in his old age and become paranoid that everyone in his household was a spy for his enemy Vu Chusker. Nuum bought Nampi's ship from Jabba, but asked him to, as a favor, assassinate Vu Chusker for him. When Jabba refused, citing he was not Nuum's henchman to order a

Name: Norba Nuum
Description: When Rusk had Jabba Desilijic Tiure locked in the dungeon for refusing to kill family enemy Vu Chusker, Norba freed him on the condition that he kill her brother for her, thereby making her the sole heir to the Nuum family's criminal empire. However after Jabba killed Rusk, Norba betrayed him, ordering her guards to take Jabba back to the dungeon for murdering her brother.

Name: Rusk Nuum
Description: When Cabrool ordered Jabba locked away in the dungeon for his refusal to kill Vu Chusker, Rusk offered to help the Hutt escape in return for killing his father, who he told Jabba had gone insane. Jabba agreed, and the next morning Cabrool Nuum was dead by Jabba's hand, leaving Rusk in command of his father's empire.

Name: Old Fourfingers
Description: The title of this article is a nickname, call sign, or alias.

Name: Pran
Description: Pran, also known as Mother Pran, was a female near-human who resided on the planet Carnelion IV.

Name: Puln
Description: In actuality, Puln working for others within the Black Sun organization. He spied on Green's operations for Vigo Sprax, but really worked for Prince Xizor, providing information on Sprax to the leader of the Black Sun.

Name: Iram Radique
Description: Iram Radique was a weapons designer who operated out of the Cog Hive Seven prison during the waning years of the Galactic Republic. In 33 BBY, the Sith Lord Darth Sidious sent his apprentice, Darth Maul, to infiltrate the prison, acquire a nuclear device from Radique, and deliver the weapon into the hands of the Bando Gora cult. Sidious planned to use the weapon to eliminate his own Master, Darth Plagueis.

Name: Reddjak
Description: Reddjak was a member of the Red Fury Brotherhood, and the captain of the Blood Brother. A pirate of near-Human blood, Reddjak worked for the Galactic Empire in its early years, but eventually operated independently around the planet of Majoor. He and his crew became celebrities, and were the inspiration for the audio drama, Space Pirates of the Galaxy. During this time, Reddjak was arrested by the Majooran authoritie

Name: Scuppa
Description: Scuppa was a part of the group Jabba took with as he went to attack Cog Hive Seven. On the Star Jewel, they successfully captured the prison ship Purge and used it to sneak into the prison.

Name: Aurra Sing
Description: Aurra Sing, also known as Nashtah, was once a female Jedi aspirant, but following a series of tragedies and misfortunes, she left the Jedi Order to become a vicious bounty hunter specializing in Jedi and political assassinations. She even became an associate in the posse of Cad Bane, a brief mentor of the orphaned Boba Fett, a commander for the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars, and later an Im

Name: Sith Mother
Description: The Sith Mother was a Force-sensitive near-human female Sith Lord who lived in the galaxy. At some point, she travelled to a planet where she met the young girl Daal, and made the girl her apprentice. The Sith Mother assigned the girl a task to venture to the region known as the Screecher's Reach and kill the resident Sith known as the Screecher. After Daal had finished the job, she met her on the planet's surface an

Name: Tionne Solusar
Description: Tionne Solusar was a Near-Human female from the planet Rindao and a Jedi Master. A scholar of Jedi history, Tionne was one of the first students of Luke Skywalker's New Jedi Order and was almost entirely responsible for regathering the lost knowledge of the Old Jedi Order. She was involved in many important events to face the order, including the Yuuzhan Vong War, and often acted as a protector for the generation of

Name: Spek-Man
Description: Spek-Man was a Near-Human mechanic, who worked for the Rebel Alliance during the Galactic Civil War. He was a member of Sleeper Cell V-16, a Rebel sleeper cell that was active on the planet Malthor around 0 BBY.

Name: Aleco Stusea
Description: Aleco Stusea was a female Near-Human Jedi Master who served on the Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council in the years after the Great Sith War. Stusea was one of several Masters who led the Jedi Enclave on the planet of Dantooine, joining the likes of Vrook Lamar, Bala Nisi, and Qual. After the official end of the Great Hunt, the Jedi Order's endeavor to eradicate Sith-created beasts galaxy-wide in the aftermath of the Grea

Name: Anavus Svag
Description: As a Jedi Guardian, Anavus was an expert on tactics, weapons, armor, and vehicles used in combat during his time. His weapon of choice was not a lightsaber, but rather a lightstaff imbued with the Force, whose ends both glowed with a resonant blue energy signature. Eventually, he recorded all his military expertise into a holocron so that future Jedi could learn from his experiences. Always answering Jedi's questions

Name: Zekka Thyne
Description: Zekka Thyne, known to some as "Patches" due to the blue mottling of his skin, was a half-Human, half-alien, who was regarded as one of the most vicious associates in the Black Sun criminal organization. Thyne was an intelligent and murderous criminal being groomed as Prince Xizor's chief associate on Corellia shortly after Black Sun started gaining power there in 7 BBY. Although rumored to be the heir-apparent to Xiz

Name: Tork
Description: As a bouncer, his job was to eject intoxicated customers who were causing trouble, or simply unwilling to leave after the bar was closed down. He was helped in this by his fierce appearance and his intimidating size (well over two meters tall).

Name: Unidentified Near-Human
Description: This individual was first shown in a flashback scene from Trill's past in Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan 2, the second issue in the The Prisoner of Bogan story arc of the Star Wars: Dawn of the Jedi comic book series.[1] Due to their face only being seen from the side, only half of the tattoo of the Aurebesh character they are named after, is seen; and there are two characters that fit the motif. Their name

Name: Unidentified near-Human mercenary
Description: A near-Human mercenary was part of a team assembled by Prince Xizor to hunt down Limna Yith and a stolen datacard on behalf of Black Sun in 3 ABY. Aboard team member Gyran's ship, the Arc Razor, they tracked Yith to the planet Sedri.

Name: Unidentified small man
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified transport captain
Description: The captain appears in "Operation: Shell Game," the adventure in Fantasy Flight Games' Star Wars: Age of Rebellion Beta, released at Gen Con Game Fair in 2013.

Name: Unidentified Zerpen Industries representative
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Woir Ur'naght
Description: Woir Ur'naght appears in Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan, a 2014 Digital Expansion to the video game Star Wars: The Old Republic, as the Level 60 Basic Gear vendor on Rishi for the Jedi Knight class His character model uses the solid red eyes that are reserved for NPCs of the Chiss species. Ur'naght and the rest of the class gear vendors at Rishi, along with all other Daily Areas and high-level planets,

Name: Saren Valek
Description: A Force-sensitive Near-Human, Saren Valek was trained in the ways of the Force by the Jedi Order, studying at the Coruscant Jedi Temple until he was selected as the Padawan to the respected Jedi Knight Alec. During Valek's apprenticeship, the boy was seduced to the dark side of the Force by the fallen Jedi Karae Nalvas in 34 BBY.[2] Valek stole Alec's blue-bladed lightsaber[1] and abandoned his former master to train

Name: Vansk
Description: Vansk was a Force-sensitive Near-Human female who became a member of the Force-using organization known as the Blackguard on the planet Mustafar. She became one of the most dangerous members of the group and was amongst its leadership[2] along with the male Human San'sii, who was the Kursk of the Blackguard while she was a member. In 1 ABY during the Galactic Civil War, a Sullustan Archaeologist called Mi Fon Lu wish

Name: Karia Ver Seryan
Description: Karia Ver Seryan was a life-bearer from Leria Kerlsil who nearly married Lando Calrissian. A life-bearer was a woman born with the mystical ability to prolong the life of an elderly or terminally ill man by linking her life essence to his in a marriage pact that doubled as a bonding of blood chemistry.

Name: Dryden Vos
Description: Dryden Vos was a near-human alien male who served as the public face of the crime syndicate Crimson Dawn during the reign of the Galactic Empire. He owned the First Light, and possessed a large quantity of wealth as Crimson Dawn's figurehead leader. He recruited the Corellian Qi'ra to be his Lieutenant, and hired Beckett's gang to steal coaxium for him. When he tried to kill Han Solo, he was betrayed by Qi'ra, and th

Name: Tira Wils
Description: Tira Wils made her name first as a mercenary, then, after leaving them, as a successful bounty hunter, calling the attention of Jir Tramsig from the Thaereian military forces in 33 BBY. One year later, recently-prometed Colonel Tramsig went to the Cularin system, and he contacted Wils to offer her employment. Among her other duties, she was the only one he trusted to meet with Qar Jalunn. Those meetings were kept sec

Name: Zeami
Description: A Force-sensitive Near-Human, Zeami was educated in the ways of the Force by the Jedi Order at the Coruscant Jedi Temple. A member of a Initiate clan under the instruction of Grand Master Yoda, Zeami befriended fellow Jedi Initiate Iguni at a young age. Selected as a Padawan by a Jedi Master, Zeami constructed a lightsaber which she would wield in battle.[1]

Name: The Great Zorbak
Description: The Great Zorbak was originally referred to by name by the Ring-Master, but this line was cut from the film and replaced with electronic music.

Name: Gern Zott
Description: Gern Zott, also known by the nickname Gerzott, was a male near-Human smuggler who lived during the latter years of the Galactic Republic. Gott operated from the modified YT-1760 transport The Panada's Kiss, and aboard the starship he carried a hollow enforcer droid shell called N4, which he rigged to a small power supply to give the illusion that the droid was fully-functional. Circa 31 BBY, Zott was hired by the Cer

Name: Dettandelles
Description: Dettandelles were a species that could wilt. In the first draft of his holobook, "Hondo's Memoirs", the pirate captain Hondo Ohnaka used the phrase "shrink like wilting dettandelles" to describe an individual being cowardly.[1]

Name: Precursor species
Description: A precursor species[2] (also precursor race)[1] was a species that eventually evolved into another or several other species. In some cases, the passage from a precursor to its descendants was accompanied by the development of sentience (as exemplified by the sentient Dowutins, whose precursors were herd animals).[2] In other cases, a species that was already sentient to begin with could give rise to several distinct

Name: Snoozbird
Description: The snoozbird was a type of creature and the inspiration behind one of the fifteen hand gestures featured in the game Five Hands.[1]

Name: Snork
Description: A snork was a kind of animal, and the namesake of one of the fifteen hand gestures in the game of Five Hands.[1]

Name: Subspecies
Description: A subspecies,[1] or race,[2] was a taxonomic subdivision of a species. Different subspecies of a species can often have different traits.[3]

Name: "Autoguard" Cybernetic Reflex Suite
Description: The "Autoguard" Cybernetic Reflex Suite was first mentioned in Fully Operational,[1] a 2018 sourcebook for the Star Wars: Age of Rebellion roleplaying game published by Fantasy Flight Games.[2]

Name: 'Ndranth Poussan
Description: 'Ndranth Poussan was a starfighter pilot in the Rebel Alliance during the Galactic Civil War. A native of the planet Agamar, Poussan joined the Alliance after a speech there by Chief of State Mon Mothma. Poussan was still undergoing training aboard the Mon Calamari Star Cruiser Independence when a shortage of pilots saw him called up for his first combat mission, providing cover for the evacuation of the Rebel base o

Name: A'mari
Description: A'mari was a clan who formerly ruled the planet of Malano III. They were distinguished by a black tattoo across their neck. When the Imperial agent X-7 infiltrated the Rebel Alliance, he pretended to be a member of the clan.
